# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_site tests/hybrid/handoff_site.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_site PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_site)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_qemu_handback tests/hybrid/qemu_handback.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_qemu_handback PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_qemu_handback)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

vsim_add_test(test_icount_trigger tests/hybrid/icount_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_icount_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_icount_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_v_regfile_xfer tests/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_v_regfile_xfer PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/../include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_v_regfile_xfer)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid_sim/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    # CSR-trigger variant (existing path, default mode).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    # COST: ctest -j greedily schedules highest-cost first to keep the
    # tail short. Tier-1 fixtures uniformly run ~15-20s on the CI host.
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST 30
    )
    # PC-trigger variant: same fixture, _hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc resolved
    # from the ELF symbol table. The csrwi insns in the binary are inert
    # in this mode (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write
    # on andes-ax45mp + AX45MPV).
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST 30
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  # Format: fname:expected_rc[:timeout_s]. Default timeout is 60 (set in
  # rt_shared_mem.sh). rt_c_matmul needs more headroom because the GEMM
  # kernel runs cycle-accurately under vsim (~45-50s) before drain.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_matmul:0:120"
    # Regression for the "no redundant ELF reload" rule: .bss survival
    # across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 without users hand-allocating fixed
    # addresses. Fails today because vsim's elf_loader and QEMU 2's
    # -kernel both zero-fill the .bss tail of every PT_LOAD.
    "rt_c_static_buf:0"
    # 1D 3-tap FIR convolution as a real RVV use case spanning the
    # handoff: QEMU1 generates x via intrinsics; vsim applies the kernel
    # (vslide1up / vslide1down + vadd) using x carried in its compiler-
    # picked V register; QEMU2 stores y and scalar-verifies. V regfile
    # transport rides on the save-all / reload-all helpers in
    # runtime/v_regfile_xfer.{h,c} so the fixture body is pure intrinsics
    # with no physical V register references.
    "rt_c_v_regs:0"
    # V scalar CSR survival: sets vxsat/vxrm/vtype/vl/vstart in vsim phase,
    # verifies in QEMU2. Exercises the full Tier-A+B restore path.
    "rt_c_v_csrs:0"
    # 16x16x16 RVV GEMM under vsim, scalar reference recompute under QEMU 2.
    # Same cycle-accurate budget headroom as rt_c_matmul (the V kernel runs
    # fewer instructions but issue/execute is implementation-dependent).
    "rt_c_v_matmul:0:120"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    list(LENGTH parts nparts)
    set(timeout_env "")
    set(tcost 60)
    if(nparts GREATER 2)
      list(GET parts 2 tsec)
      set(timeout_env "TIMEOUT=${tsec} ")
      # Use the per-fixture timeout as the scheduling cost so long
      # fixtures (rt_c_matmul: 120s) start in the first wave under -j.
      set(tcost ${tsec})
    endif()
    # CSR variant.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=csr FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-csr"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
    # PC variant: same fixture, same timeout, mode flipped.
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}_pc
      COMMAND bash -c
        "${timeout_env}TRIGGER_MODE=pc FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname}_pc PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e;hybrid-e2e-pc"
      COST ${tcost}
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer, tty-induced SIGTTOU).
  # No PC variants - these assert orchestrator failure modes that are
  # orthogonal to the trigger mechanism.
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout pty_handback)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Contract test: QEMU plugin exits 200 on drain (matches vsim's
  # kExitHandoffToQemu). Guards the F1 rebrand from regressing back to
  # exit(0), which would make workload-completion indistinguishable from
  # a drained handoff under the orchestrator.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_drain_exit_code
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_drain_exit_code.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_drain_exit_code PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F5: plugin icount-trigger mode. Runs a CSR-less spin fixture with
  # icount=N; asserts drain at exit 200 and minstret >= N.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_icount_trigger
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_icount_trigger.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_icount_trigger PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F7: plugin BBV-emit mode (SimPoint profile). Runs the CSR-less spin
  # with bbv_out= + slice= + icount=; asserts the .bb file has the
  # expected row count and is consumable by tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_bbv_emit
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_bbv_emit.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_bbv_emit PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # F11: plugin BBV slice-consume mode. Runs the CSR-less spin with
  # slice= + slice_at=K + icount=fallback; asserts drain at the end of
  # slice index K (0-based), before the icount fallback fires.
  add_test(NAME test_plugin_phase_consume
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/test_plugin_phase_consume.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_plugin_phase_consume PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )
endif()

content.content:
1 # How the Hybrid Simulator Works 2 3 A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. 4 5 ## The Problem in One Paragraph 6 7 A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. 8 9 The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. 10 11 ## High-Level Concept 12 13 Five big ideas make this possible: 14 15 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 16 17 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 18 19 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 20 21 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 22 23 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. 24 25 ## State Categories and Their Transport 26 27 The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. 28 29 | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | 30 |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| 31 | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | 32 | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | 33 | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | 34 | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | 35 | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | 36 | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | 37 | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | 38 | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | 39 | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | 40 | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | 41 42 "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. 43 44 ## Trigger Modes 45 46 Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. 47 48 | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | 49 |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| 50 | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | 51 | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | 52 53 Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): 54 55 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. 56 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. 57 - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. 58 59 ## Design Rationale 60 61 **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). 62 63 **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. 64 65 **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. 66 67 **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. 68 69 **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. 70 71 **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. 72 73 **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. 74 75 ## Architecture Flowchart 76 77 ```mermaid 78 flowchart LR 79 subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] 80 SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] 81 STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] 82 STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] 83 end 84 85 subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] 86 Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] 87 Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] 88 Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN 89 end 90 91 subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] 92 V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] 93 V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] 94 V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] 95 V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] 96 V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE 97 V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG 98 V_TRIG --> V_CTRL 99 V_CTRL --> V_DM 100 end 101 102 subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] 103 Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] 104 Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] 105 Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG 106 end 107 108 Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q 109 Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM 110 STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL 111 STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM 112 V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM 113 SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM 114 115 V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V 116 V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 117 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB 118 119 Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 120 V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 121 Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM 122 ``` 123 124 ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test 125 126 The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. 127 128 Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: 129 130 - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. 131 - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. 132 133 ### What each phase does 134 135 ``` 136 Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) 137 ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- 138 seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) 139 csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches 140 vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) 141 csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 142 ``` 143 144 ### State that crosses each handoff 145 146 | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | 147 |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| 148 | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | 149 | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | 150 | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | 151 152 ### What each step proves 153 154 | Step | What it tests | 155 |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| 156 | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | 157 | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | 158 | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | 159 | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | 160 161 A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. 162 163 ## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For 164 165 You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists. 166 167 ### 1. Phase 2 (vsim) must be syscall-free; phase 1 and 3 (QEMU) get standard newlib 168 169 C fixtures link against the Andes toolchain's virtual-hosting libgloss (`-lgloss_vh`), whose `_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/... all bottom out in the RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Both QEMU launches enable semihosting (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`), so the triple is intercepted before reaching `mtvec`. vsim does NOT implement semihosting -- inside the ROI the triple routes to `mtvec` -> `m_trap_entry` -> trap loop. 170 171 | Phase | Standard newlib I/O (`printf`, `fopen`, ...) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak | What you can do | 172 |---------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| 173 | QEMU 1 | works (via `-lgloss_vh` -> semihosting) | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) | full newlib stdio: `printf`, `puts`, `fwrite`, etc. | 174 | vsim | TRAPS (newlib stdio -> `_write` -> semihosting ebreak -> `mtvec`) | not implemented -> trap loop | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no syscalls, no `_exit`. | 175 | QEMU 2 | works (same `-lgloss_vh` path) | enabled | full newlib stdio. | 176 177 The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) marks the boundary: 178 179 ```c 180 // ===== QEMU 1 window ===== // printf etc. are fine 181 enter_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -> hand off to vsim 182 // ===== vsim window ===== // syscall-free: registers + memory only 183 exit_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -> hand off to QEMU 2 184 // ===== QEMU 2 window ===== // printf etc. are fine again 185 ``` 186 187 The forbidden-ness in phase 2 is transitive: anything that ultimately calls `_write`/`_exit`/`_open`/... (i.e. anything that emits via the libgloss syscall stubs) is out. Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, ``, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3." 188 189 There is no third channel; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.** 190 191 ### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation 192 193 vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely: 194 195 - Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost. 196 - Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost. 197 - Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost. 198 - Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins). 199 200 If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C. 201 202 ### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates 203 204 `v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes. 205 206 `rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example. 207 208 ### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains 209 210 ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop. 211 212 ### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi` 213 214 The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it. 215 216 ### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window 217 218 Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts: 219 220 - `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`). 221 - `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`). 222 - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code). 223 - Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`. 224 225 Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim. 226 227 ### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer 228 229 Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them. 230 231 ### Where each rule lives in the runtime 232 233 | Concern | File | 234 |-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| 235 | Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address) | `runtime/rt_c.ld` | 236 | `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear | `runtime/crt0.S` | 237 | M-mode trap save/restore + fatal trap reporter | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` | 238 | Newlib syscall stubs (`_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/...) via semihosting | `-lgloss_vh` from the Andes toolchain | 239 | Phase markers (`enter_vsim`, `exit_vsim`) | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` | 240 | `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) | 241 | QEMU launch flags (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_shared_mem}.sh` (QEMU 1), `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (QEMU 2) | 242 | C-fixture build line (`-nodefaultlibs -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc`) | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` | 243 244 For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`. 245 246 ## Known Limitations 247 248 These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list. 249 250 ### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped 251 252 **What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind. 253 254 **Affected registers:** 255 - All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`). 256 - 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped. 257 - All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`). 258 - `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target). 259 260 **Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP. 261 262 **Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase. 263 264 **Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block. 265 266 ### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff 267 268 Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording. 269 270 ### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs 271 272 The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug. 273 274 ### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined 275 276 Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. vsim's only deterministic peripheral is shared memory (the `mmap` backing file); there is no console, no clock, no semihosting. Stick to register-and-memory work inside the ROI. 277 278 ### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported 279 280 The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it. 281 282 ### L6. Single hart only (today) 283 284 The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end. 285 286 ## Where to Look Next 287 288 - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. 289 - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. 290 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. 291 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. 292 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. 293 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. 294 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. 295 - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. 296 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. 297 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap reporter, phase-aware drain markers. Newlib syscall stubs come from the toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting), not from a project-local libgloss. 298 - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - per-fixture build rules; documents which `-march`/linker-script combination each tier uses. 299 - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. 300 301 ## Mental Model in One Sentence 302 303 > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2. 304

file.content:
# How the Hybrid Simulator Works A friendly tour for engineers new to this codebase. ## The Problem in One Paragraph A pure RTL simulator (`vsim`, built on Verilator + SystemC) is **cycle-accurate**: it tells you exactly how many cycles every instruction takes inside the CPU pipeline. The price is speed: it runs at ~5-9 KHz. Booting Linux, running libc init, and warming up before reaching the actual code you care about can take hours. Meanwhile, QEMU runs the same program **functionally** at MHz speed, but it has no notion of pipeline cycles. The hybrid simulator combines them: run the boring parts in QEMU, switch to vsim for the small region of interest (ROI) you actually want to measure, and (optionally) hand back to QEMU to finish. ## High-Level Concept Five big ideas make this possible: 1. **Memory is shared, not copied.** Both simulators `mmap` the same backing file (`/dev/shm/...`) as their RAM. When QEMU writes a byte at `0x80001000`, vsim sees it instantly, and vice versa. RAM size becomes irrelevant. 2. **All architectural state fits in one packed struct.** GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, ...), PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr), V scalar CSRs (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr, vlenb), and the full V regfile (`v0..v31`, up to 2 KB) all live in `struct hybrid_state_v1` (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`). That struct is the wire contract every component agrees on. 3. **vsim only refreshes what the ROI can mutate.** vsim restores into the hart only what it needs to run (GPRs + PC + V state); on the way out it drains back only what could have changed (GPRs + PC + V state). Everything else (FP, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regfile bytes when the ROI doesn't touch them) **passes through unchanged** from the QEMU 1 snapshot to the QEMU 2 restore. This skips ~85+ DMI transactions per handoff. 4. **The trigger is a magic CSR write or a PC match.** `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` enters vsim; `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` exits it. CSR `0x7C0` is in the user-defined range. A third form, `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1`, decides at runtime based on `rs1`'s value (1 = exit). PC-trigger mode skips the magic CSR entirely - the orchestrator names entry/exit PCs on the CLI. 5. **Microarchitectural state is thrown away.** Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer - none of it transfers. We accept cold caches at handoff and rely on a "warm-up window" before recording. ## State Categories and Their Transport The wire struct is uniform; transport per category depends on what QEMU's plugin API and the AX45MPV Debug Module are willing to expose. | Category | QEMU 1 -> snapshot | snapshot -> vsim hart | vsim hart -> snapshot | snapshot -> QEMU 2 | |---------------------------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|--------------------| | GPRs `x1..x31`, PC | TCG plugin reads | DM Abstract Commands (DPC for PC) | DM Abstract Commands (fresh read) | gdbstub `G` packet | | `mtvec` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command | passthrough (not re-read) | gdbstub `P` | | Other M-mode CSRs (`mstatus`, `mepc`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `satp`) | TCG plugin | passthrough (not written) | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `kCsrByName`) | | FPRs `f0..f31` | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (per `hybrid_fpr_names`) | | PMP (16 cfg + 64 addr) | TCG plugin | passthrough | passthrough | gdbstub `P` (each slot) | | V scalar CSRs - `vtype`, `vl` | TCG plugin | DM Program Buffer running `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V scalar CSRs - `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, `mstatus.VS` | TCG plugin | DM Abstract Command, **with Program-Buffer fallback** if the DM rejects | DM Abstract Command (fresh read) | gdbstub `P` | | V regfile `v0..v31` (up to 2 KB) | TCG plugin | shared-mmap scratch region (`hybrid::kVScratchGpa = 0x80FF0000`); fixture executes `vle8.v` to load | fixture executes `vse8.v` back to scratch; vsim memcpys those bytes into `state.vreg[]` | gdbstub `P` (per V-reg index) | | `vlenb` | TCG plugin | not restored (read-only, fixed by NDS_VLEN) | passthrough (verified, not re-read) | not sent | | Guest memory | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | -- (in shared mmap) | "Passthrough" means: vsim copies the field from the inbound snapshot into the outbound snapshot unchanged. The hart never sees it; the field rides from QEMU 1 directly to QEMU 2. ## Trigger Modes Two ways to delimit the ROI; pick at runtime via `TRIGGER_MODE=csr|pc`. | Mode | Entry into vsim | Exit out of vsim | Source of truth | |------|------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|-----------------| | CSR | `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==0`) | `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (or `csrrw rd, 0x7C0, rs1` with `rs1==1`) | the binary itself | | PC | plugin arg `enter_pc=0x...` (no insn match) | vsim CLI `--hybrid-exit-pc 0x...` (synthetic hit added via `CsrTrigger::add_pc_hit`) | the orchestrator/CLI; binary is unchanged | Three handoff kinds (`hybrid_handoff_kind` in `include/hybrid/insn_match.h`): - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_ENTER` - QEMU drains and exits; vsim ignores at retire. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT` - vsim drains and exits; QEMU does not encounter this PC. - `HYBRID_HANDOFF_RUNTIME` - vsim decodes `rs1` from the matched insn, reads its current GPR value, fires only when it equals 1. Lets a single binary parameterize entry vs exit at runtime. ## Design Rationale **One packed struct is the wire format.** Three components in three languages (QEMU plugin in C, vsim in C++, gdbstub-translation layer in C++) need to agree byte-for-byte on what state means. A C struct in a shared header (`include/hybrid/state_abi.h`) is the simplest contract that compiles into all three. Versioning is via `magic` + `version`, with `reserved` bytes for forward growth (vxsat/vxrm/vcsr were added by consuming `reserved` rather than bumping to v2). **QEMU 1 -> vsim is file-based, vsim -> QEMU 2 is gdbstub-based.** QEMU mainline is used **as-is** (no patches, no fork). It exposes two relevant extension points: the TCG plugin API can *read* state during execution, and gdbstub `-S` can *write* state before the first fetch. Plugins cannot inject state at startup, and there is no `--load-state-from-file` flag. Each direction picks the only working primitive: the plugin drains to a file out of QEMU 1, and gdbstub `G`/`P` packets seed QEMU 2. The vsim side translates between the file and the RSP packets - QEMU never has to know about `hybrid_state_v1`. **Passthrough is the largest performance lever.** A naive design would round-trip every field of `hybrid_state_v1` through DM Abstract Commands - hundreds of DMI transactions. Instead, `HandoffController::set_passthrough_input(snapshot)` keeps QEMU 1's drained state in vsim memory; when the exit trigger fires, `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough(snapshot)` re-reads only x1..x31 + PC + (when V) the six V CSRs via DM, and merges those over the snapshot. Everything the ROI is *not allowed* to touch (FPRs, M-mode CSRs except mtvec, PMP) flows from QEMU 1 straight into the QEMU 2 gdbstub restore. ROI-mutates-FP would silently lose those changes - that's an explicit, documented limitation, not an oversight. **The Debug Module handles most ISA registers; `vtype`/`vl` need the Program Buffer.** GPRs, FPRs, and ordinary CSRs all have abstract-command slots in the AX45MPV DM - one DMI transaction each. `vtype` is the exception: the V spec marks it read-only via direct CSR write because writing it sets the `vill` flag and invalidates the vector unit. The only legal restore for `vtype` + `vl` together is the `vsetvl` instruction, which has to *execute on the hart*. The Program Buffer is exactly that slot: stage `vl` and `vtype` in scratch GPRs (x5/x6), then execute `vsetvl x0, x5, x6` in the PB. The GPR loop afterwards overwrites x5/x6, so no leakage. **Tier-A V CSRs use Abstract-first with a Program-Buffer fallback.** `vstart`, `vxsat`, `vxrm`, `vcsr`, and `mstatus.VS` are spec-compliant to direct CSR write, but the AX45MPV DM may reject the abstract path on a given build. `ResumeDriver::write_csr_abstract_or_pb` tries Abstract first, checks `abstractcs.cmderr`, and on failure clears the sticky error and falls back to a one-instruction PB sequence (`csrrw x0, csr, x5`). The fallback is silent and per-CSR, so a partially-implemented DM does not block the rest of the restore. **The V regfile rides shared mmap, not the Debug Module.** The RISC-V Debug Spec does not allocate abstract-command slots for `v0..v31`; it leaves V to optional Program Buffer or DPI extensions, and the AX45MPV wrapper does not implement either. Pushing 32 x 64-byte regs through the Program Buffer one element at a time would be both slow and intricate. Since both simulators already share an mmap, we reuse it: vsim copies `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region before `dret`, and the fixture's vsim-phase prologue issues `vle8.v` from that region to load the real V regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's epilogue issues `vse8.v` to scratch, and the simulator wrapper reads those bytes back into `state.vreg[]` after `StateDrain` returns. The fixture is aware of the contract; the V regfile reuses the mmap that already exists for general memory. **Missing-register tolerance is part of the contract.** A given QEMU build may not advertise every PMP slot, and the DM may not implement every CSR. `apply_state` in `qemu_handback.hpp` silently skips any name not in the discovered RegMap (`try_write_named`); `StateDrain::read_64bit_register` returns 0 and clears `cmderr` when the DM rejects. Failing closed would block bring-up on every QEMU/RTL revision; failing soft keeps the happy path moving and surfaces real bugs at the fixture level. ## Architecture Flowchart ```mermaid flowchart LR subgraph DISK["Shared backing files"] SHM[("/dev/shm RAM
(mmap shared)
+ V scratch
at 0x80FF0000")] STATE_Q["state_qemu.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] STATE_V["state_vsim.bin
hybrid_state_v1"] end subgraph QEMU1["QEMU 1 (functional, MHz)"] Q1_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q1_PLUGIN["hybrid_handoff.so
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,0
PC mode: enter_pc=0x..."] Q1_TCG --> Q1_PLUGIN end subgraph VSIM["vsim (cycle-accurate, KHz)"] V_PIPE["RTL pipeline
(Verilator + SystemC)"] V_DM["JTAG DM driver (in-process)
Abstract Cmds + Program Buffer"] V_TRIG["CsrTrigger
CSR mode: csrwi 0x7C0,1 (or runtime form)
PC mode: --hybrid-exit-pc"] V_CTRL["HandoffController
passthrough(snapshot) +
fresh GPR/PC/V-CSR drain"] V_DM -.GPR/FPR/CSR + vsetvl.-> V_PIPE V_PIPE -.retire signals.-> V_TRIG V_TRIG --> V_CTRL V_CTRL --> V_DM end subgraph QEMU2["QEMU 2 (functional, MHz)"] Q2_GDB["gdbstub -gdb tcp::PORT -S"] Q2_TCG["TCG insn loop"] Q2_GDB -.G + per-name P packets.-> Q2_TCG end Q1_PLUGIN -- "drain ALL state" --> STATE_Q Q1_PLUGIN -- "qemu_plugin_request_exit" --> VSIM STATE_Q -- "--resume-from-qemu (passthrough seed)" --> V_CTRL STATE_Q -- "ResumeDriver: GPR/PC/mtvec/V" --> V_DM V_DM -.stage V regs.-> SHM SHM -.drain V regs.-> V_DM V_CTRL -- "merge fresh + passthrough" --> STATE_V V_TRIG -- "exit code 200" --> QEMU2 STATE_V -- "RSP G + P packets" --> Q2_GDB Q1_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM V_PIPE <-->|"every load/store"| SHM Q2_TCG <-->|"every load/store"| SHM ``` ## Walk-Through: `rt_c_v_regs` E2E Test The canonical demo: it exercises **memory** transport (via shared mmap), **GPR / scalar CSR** transport (via the state file + DM Abstract Commands), and **V regfile** transport (via the V-scratch trick) all in one round-trip. Source: `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`. Two memory regions live in the shared mmap: - `SEED` at `0x80100000` - 16 int32 elements, the input pattern. - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` - 2 KB region, the V regfile transport channel. ### What each phase does ``` Phase 1 (QEMU 1) Phase 2 (vsim) Phase 3 (QEMU 2) ----------------- --------------------------------- -------------------- seed[0..15] = pattern vle32.v v8..v15, (seed) vse32.v v8..v15, (out) csrwi 0x7C0, 0 vadd.vi v9, v9, +100 verify out matches vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH) seed (with v9 +100) csrwi 0x7C0, 1 SEMIHOST_EXIT 0 ``` ### State that crosses each handoff | Boundary | Memory | Fresh-read in vsim | Passthrough through vsim | Fixture-mediated (mmap scratch) | |--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------| | QEMU 1 -> vsim | `SEED` already in shared mmap; vsim sees it instantly | (nothing yet) | Snapshot held by HandoffController; mtvec written by ResumeDriver | `state.vreg[]` (zero) staged into V_SCRATCH | | in vsim | `vle32` reads SEED; `vse8` writes V regs to V_SCRATCH | -- | -- | -- | | vsim -> QEMU 2 | `SEED` unchanged | x1..x31, PC, 6 V CSRs (DM) | FP, M-mode CSRs (except mtvec written above), PMP, vlenb (all from QEMU 1) | `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` per V-reg | ### What each step proves | Step | What it tests | |----------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | Phase 1 fills `SEED` via int32 stores | QEMU 1's RAM backend is the shared file (not a private anonymous mapping). | | `vle32.v v8..v15, (seed)` runs in vsim | **Memory written in QEMU survives into vsim**, AND `mstatus.VS` + `vtype/vl` were correctly re-applied by ResumeDriver (otherwise `vsetvli` would have trapped). | | Phase 3 `vse32.v` + verify pass | **V regfile written in vsim survives into QEMU 2.** The `V_SCRATCH` -> `read_v_from_scratch` -> `state.vreg[]` -> gdbstub `P` chain works. `v9 == seed + 100` confirms the *mutated* value, not zero-init or stale snapshot. | | Final exit code 0 | Full round-trip succeeded: shared memory, fresh GPRs, fresh V CSRs, V regfile, and passthrough fields all delivered intact to QEMU 2. | A sibling fixture, `rt_c_v_csrs.c`, exercises the V *scalar* CSR survival in isolation (vtype, vl, vstart, vxsat, vxrm, vcsr) using the same three-phase shape but no V regfile traffic. ## Authoring a Fixture: What to Watch Out For You are writing **one** ELF whose code body straddles three execution windows: QEMU 1 (pre-ROI), vsim (the ROI itself), QEMU 2 (post-ROI). Each window exposes a different set of host-call peripherals and accepts a different subset of state mutations. The constraints below are the ones that bite first-time fixture authors. Each rule is followed by the reason it exists. ### 1. Phase 2 (vsim) must be syscall-free; phase 1 and 3 (QEMU) get standard newlib C fixtures link against the Andes toolchain's virtual-hosting libgloss (`-lgloss_vh`), whose `_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/... all bottom out in the RISC-V semihosting magic triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Both QEMU launches enable semihosting (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`), so the triple is intercepted before reaching `mtvec`. vsim does NOT implement semihosting -- inside the ROI the triple routes to `mtvec` -> `m_trap_entry` -> trap loop. | Phase | Standard newlib I/O (`printf`, `fopen`, ...) | RISC-V semihosting magic ebreak | What you can do | |---------|----------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------| | QEMU 1 | works (via `-lgloss_vh` -> semihosting) | enabled (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`) | full newlib stdio: `printf`, `puts`, `fwrite`, etc. | | vsim | TRAPS (newlib stdio -> `_write` -> semihosting ebreak -> `mtvec`) | not implemented -> trap loop | **register / memory only**. No `printf`, no syscalls, no `_exit`. | | QEMU 2 | works (same `-lgloss_vh` path) | enabled | full newlib stdio. | The runtime (`rt_c_helpers.h`) marks the boundary: ```c // ===== QEMU 1 window ===== // printf etc. are fine enter_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 0 -> hand off to vsim // ===== vsim window ===== // syscall-free: registers + memory only exit_vsim(); // csrwi 0x7C0, 1 -> hand off to QEMU 2 // ===== QEMU 2 window ===== // printf etc. are fine again ``` The forbidden-ness in phase 2 is transitive: anything that ultimately calls `_write`/`_exit`/`_open`/... (i.e. anything that emits via the libgloss syscall stubs) is out. Pure CPU functions are fine: `vsnprintf` into a buffer, `memcpy`, ``, inline asm including V instructions, `setjmp`/`longjmp`. The pattern for cross-phase output is "compute in phase 2, emit in phase 1 or 3." There is no third channel; **plan the phase your I/O goes in.** ### 2. Only GPRs / PC / V-state survive vsim mutation vsim refreshes only `x1..x31` + PC + (when V is enabled) the six V CSRs out of the hart at the exit trigger. Everything else - **FPRs, M-mode CSRs except `mtvec`, PMP, `vlenb`** - rides along untouched in QEMU 1's snapshot. Concretely: - Mutating `f3` inside vsim: QEMU 2 sees QEMU 1's value of `f3`. Silently lost. - Mutating `mscratch`, `mip`, `mie`, `satp`, ... inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating PMP inside vsim: silently lost. - Mutating `mtvec`: lost (vsim writes its own mtvec back in but doesn't drain it; the snapshot wins). If your test depends on a non-GPR mutation propagating to QEMU 2, you have to mutate it from the QEMU 2 side instead - e.g. set the value into a memory cell during vsim, then `csrw` from it during Phase C. ### 3. The V regfile only round-trips if the fixture cooperates `v0..v31` doesn't ride DM Abstract Commands. Vsim memcpys QEMU 1's `state.vreg[]` into a fixed scratch region (`V_SCRATCH = 0x80FF0000`, 2 KB) on resume; the fixture's vsim-phase prologue must `vle8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` to load those bytes into the real regfile. Drain mirrors: the fixture's vsim-phase epilogue must `vse8.v v0..v31, (V_SCRATCH)` before the exit trigger, otherwise `state.vreg[]` reaches QEMU 2 with stale (or zero) bytes. `rt_c_v_regs.c` is the worked example. ### 4. `mstatus.VS` (and `.FS`) must already be set when QEMU 1 drains ResumeDriver writes `mstatus` to the vsim hart **only** if the QEMU 1 snapshot has VS bits non-zero (`state.mstatus & 0x600u`). The shared `runtime/crt0.S` runs before the QEMU 1 drain marker and sets `mstatus.VS=Initial` and `mstatus.FS=Initial`. **If you skip the standard crt0**, set both yourself before `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Otherwise vsim resumes with `VS=Off` and the very first `vsetvli` in your ROI raises illegal-insn -> trap loop. ### 5. The trigger instruction must be 4-byte `csrwi` The plugin filters on a 32-bit insn (`size != 4` is rejected). Don't let the assembler emit a compressed form. The runtime helpers wrap the asm in inline strings; if you write the trigger by hand, force `.option norvc` around it. ### 6. Stay inside the shared 128 MB RAM window Default mmap window: `0x80000000..0x88000000` (QEMU virt's RAM base + 128 MB). The runtime puts: - `.text/.rodata/.data/.bss` at `0x80000000` upward (`runtime/rt_c.ld`). - `_stack` at `0x80700000` (override with `-Wl,--defsym,_stack=...`). - `V_SCRATCH` at `0x80FF0000` (must not be touched by your code). - Heap (`_sbrk`) growing from `_end`. Anything between `0x80FF0000` and `0x88000000` is yours; above `0x88000000` is unmapped in QEMU 1 unless you also pass a larger `-m`. Below `0x80000000` is MMIO/ROM in QEMU virt and undefined in vsim. ### 7. Microarchitectural state does not transfer Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, lr/sc reservations - all dropped at each handoff. Don't rely on a `lr.d` that crosses the entry trigger; it will not see its `sc.d` partner. `mcycle`/`minstret` are not continuous either - keep that in mind if your test reads them. ### Where each rule lives in the runtime | Concern | File | |-------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------| | Memory map (`_stack`, `_end`, load address) | `runtime/rt_c.ld` | | `mstatus.{FS,VS}` init, sp/gp, `.bss` clear | `runtime/crt0.S` | | M-mode trap save/restore + fatal trap reporter | `runtime/trap.S` + `runtime/handler.c` | | Newlib syscall stubs (`_write`/`_exit`/`_sbrk`/...) via semihosting | `-lgloss_vh` from the Andes toolchain | | Phase markers (`enter_vsim`, `exit_vsim`) | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` | | `_hybrid_{enter,exit}_pc` labels for PC-trigger mode | `runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (resolved by `run_e2e.sh` via `nm`) | | QEMU launch flags (`-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, `-cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512`) | `tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e,rt_shared_mem}.sh` (QEMU 1), `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (QEMU 2) | | C-fixture build line (`-nodefaultlibs -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc`) | `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` | For pure-asm fixtures (`handoff_roundtrip.S`, `rt_all_gprs.S`, ...) the runtime is skipped entirely: link with `handoff_roundtrip.ld` and inline `semihost_exit.inc` for the QEMU 2 exit. Rules 1, 2, 4-7 still apply; you just hand-code the same checks instead of relying on `runtime/`. ## Known Limitations These are properties of the current implementation that fixture authors hit even when they follow every rule above. Each one has a documented workaround or is on a future-work list. ### L1. vsim mutations to FPRs, M-mode CSRs (except `mtvec`), and PMP are silently dropped **What this means.** If your ROI executes `fadd f3, ...`, `csrw mscratch, ...`, or modifies any `pmpcfg*`/`pmpaddr*` slot inside vsim, the value reaching QEMU 2 is **whatever QEMU 1 had at the entry trigger**, not what vsim left behind. **Affected registers:** - All 32 FPRs (`f0..f31`). - 12 of the 13 M-mode CSRs in the wire struct: `mstatus`, `mepc`, `mcause`, `mtval`, `mscratch`, `medeleg`, `mideleg`, `mip`, `mie`, `mcycle`, `minstret`, `satp`. Only `mtvec` is round-tripped. - All PMP entries (16 `pmpcfg`, 64 `pmpaddr`). - `vlenb` (read-only constant; not really a mutation target). **Why.** `StateDrain::drain_with_passthrough` re-reads only `x1..x31`, PC, and the six V scalar CSRs via DM Abstract Commands; the rest is copied through from QEMU 1's snapshot. This is a deliberate speed optimisation (~85 fewer DMI transactions per handoff) under the assumption that ROI kernels don't touch FP / M-CSRs / PMP. **Workaround.** If your test must depend on a non-GPR mutation, route it through memory: `csrr` the value into a GPR, store to a known address, then `csrw` from that address inside QEMU 2's phase. **Permanent fix.** Extend `state_drain.hpp::drain_with_passthrough` to fresh-read the relevant fields via Abstract Commands, mirroring the existing V-CSR block. ### L2. Microarchitectural state is dropped at every handoff Caches, TLB, branch predictor, store buffer, and **lr/sc reservations** are not transferred. A `lr.d` in QEMU 1 paired with `sc.d` in vsim will not see its reservation. Cycle-accurate measurements taken inside the ROI will see cold caches at entry and again at exit; use a warm-up window before recording. ### L3. `mcycle` / `minstret` are not continuous across handoffs The cycle and instret counters jump at each transition (each simulator runs them on its own time base). For a monotonic stream, sum across phases manually; treat the gap as a documented discontinuity rather than a bug. ### L4. MMIO inside the ROI is undefined Touching device registers (UART, PLIC, CLINT, peripheral DMA, etc.) inside the vsim window is unsupported - the cycle-accurate interconnect may not even instantiate the device QEMU 1 was talking to. vsim's only deterministic peripheral is shared memory (the `mmap` backing file); there is no console, no clock, no semihosting. Stick to register-and-memory work inside the ROI. ### L5. Self-modifying code across the trigger is not supported The magic CSR write is a synchronization boundary by convention. Rewriting code bytes that QEMU 1 has already executed but vsim is about to fetch (or vice-versa) is undefined: caches and translation buffers are dropped, but the underlying mmap is shared, so timing is the only thing keeping you out of trouble. Don't do it. ### L6. Single hart only (today) The wire struct carries `hart_id` and the plumbing is per-hart-aware, but the resume/drain path has only been exercised on hart 0. Multi-hart fixtures are not yet supported end-to-end. ## Where to Look Next - `include/hybrid/state_abi.h` - the wire contract. - `qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c` - QEMU drain side. See `resolve_handles` (lazy because the dynamic gdb coprocessors aren't registered at vcpu_init) and the entry-trigger callback. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/handoff_controller.hpp` - passthrough wiring and trigger dispatch. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` - GPR/PC/mtvec + V-CSR Tier-A (Abstract w/ PB fallback) + Tier-B (`vsetvl` via PB) restore order. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` - DM fresh-read of GPRs/PC/V-CSRs; passthrough merge. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/program_buffer.hpp` - PB exec helper, `encode_vsetvl`, `encode_csrrw`. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/v_regfile_xfer.hpp` - V-scratch staging / drain helpers. - `hybrid_vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` - spawning QEMU 2, RegMap discovery via `qXfer:features:read`, and the `apply_state` template that ships every category to the gdbstub. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.c`, `rt_c_v_csrs.c` - the V regfile and V scalar CSR fixtures. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{rt_c.ld, crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, rt_c_helpers.h}` - the shared C runtime: linker script, entry, trap reporter, phase-aware drain markers. Newlib syscall stubs come from the toolchain's `-lgloss_vh` (semihosting), not from a project-local libgloss. - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` - per-fixture build rules; documents which `-march`/linker-script combination each tier uses. - `run_e2e.sh` - run all e2e tests locally without going through CMake/CTest. ## Mental Model in One Sentence > Two simulators agree on a memory map and a register struct, take turns running the same program, and use a magic CSR write (or a PC match) as the baton-pass. vsim only refreshes the parts the ROI is allowed to mutate (GPRs, PC, V state); everything else rides along in the snapshot from QEMU 1 straight to QEMU 2.

questions.question: What scope should the E2E test plan target?

options.label: Coverage of state that round-trips today + passthrough guards (Recommended)
options.description: Focus on fields TODAY observed by drain (x1..x31, PC, V-state, mtvec) plus 'passthrough integrity' guards for FP/M-CSR/PMP. Lighter on Phase 5 work that hasn't been built yet.

options.label: Plus pre-emptive Phase 5 tests (FP/M-CSR/PMP round-trip, multi-hart, trap-in-ROI)
options.description: Include fixtures that will only PASS after Phase 5 hardening lands. Each is RED today and becomes GREEN as the underlying drain path is extended.

options.label: Full coverage + performance regression (wall-clock vs fully-RTL baseline)
options.description: Adds benchmark fixtures asserting >=100x speedup target from hybrid_plan.md Phase 4. Requires a fully-RTL baseline run for byte-identical kanata comparison.

questions.question: How should the plan be organized?

options.label: By RISK category: fresh-read fidelity, passthrough integrity, trigger paths, failure modes (Recommended)
options.description: Each section maps to a class of bug the suite is designed to detect. Easy to spot coverage gaps. Matches how hybrid_plan.md Section 8 already structured Phase 3 e2e work.

options.label: By STATE class: GPR, FPR, M-CSR, PMP, V-state, memory, PC
options.description: One section per field family in hybrid_state_v1. Mirrors the ABI struct layout; simpler 1:1 mapping but doesn't surface trigger/negative/multi-roundtrip dimensions cleanly.

options.label: By PHASE/RUNNER: unit -> Tier-1 -> Tier-2 -> negative -> plugin-feature
options.description: Group by where the test lives in the build (unit ctests, roundtrip_e2e.sh, rt_shared_mem.sh, negative drivers, plugin smoke). Matches the directory layout but obscures coverage intent.
