One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: in-progress
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; weights sum to 1.0 +/- 1e-3; prints chosen
  slice indices.

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 end-to-end + M8)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Reads simpts from T4 output (or env `SLICE_AT=K`); plugin
  `slice=1000,slice_at=K`; vsim resume + drain; QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: T4 (for simpts) OR explicit SLICE_AT env. F11 (plugin
  slice-consume).
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body; QEMU2 exit 0.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root
  $LOG/snap`; assert `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist + validate
  schema; then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with vsim`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; post-replay state.bin differs from
  archive (PC advanced).

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

toolUseResult.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T1: CSR insn-match switching mode (M1) + end-of-program (M8).
#
# Walks one round-trip with CSR triggers (csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1}) on a
# state-rich Tier-2 fixture (rt_c_v_regs). Demonstrates the default
# switching path a user gets out of the box.
#
# Honors all the same env overrides as rt_shared_mem.sh (VSIM_BIN,
# VSIM_QEMU, VSIM_PLUGIN, VSIM_ELF, NM, TIMEOUT).

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="CSR insn match (M1+M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: csrwi 0x7C0, 0 (enter) -> csrwi 0x7C0, 1 (exit)"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=csr EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M1] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M1] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; weights sum to 1.0 +/- 1e-3; prints chosen
  slice indices.

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 end-to-end + M8)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Reads simpts from T4 output (or env `SLICE_AT=K`); plugin
  `slice=1000,slice_at=K`; vsim resume + drain; QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: T4 (for simpts) OR explicit SLICE_AT env. F11 (plugin
  slice-consume).
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body; QEMU2 exit 0.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root
  $LOG/snap`; assert `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist + validate
  schema; then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with vsim`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; post-replay state.bin differs from
  archive (PC advanced).

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

**If the interval doesn't cleanly divide its unit** (e.g. `7m` → `*/7 * * * *` gives uneven gaps at :56→:00; `90m` → 1.5h which cron can't express), pick the nearest clean interval and tell the user what you rounded to before scheduling.

Then:
1. Call CronCreate with: `cron` (the expression above), `prompt` (the parsed prompt verbatim), `recurring: true`.
2. Briefly confirm: what's scheduled, the cron expression, the human-readable cadence, that recurring tasks auto-expire after 7 days, and that the user can cancel sooner with CronDelete (include the job ID). Only if you did NOT show the cloud-offer AskUserQuestion above (i.e., neither trigger condition applied), end the confirmation with this exact line on its own, italicized: `_Runs until you close this session · For durable cloud-based loops, use /schedule_`. If the user already answered that question, omit this line.
3. **Then immediately execute the parsed prompt now** — don't wait for the first cron fire. If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.

## Dynamic mode (rule 3 — no interval)

The user wants you to self-pace. Decide what makes the next iteration worth running — a passage of time, or an observable event.

1. **Run the parsed prompt now.** If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.
2. **If the next run is gated on an event** (CI finishing, a log line matching, a file changing, a PR comment) and no Monitor is already running for it: arm one now with `persistent: true`. Its events arrive as `<task-notification>` messages and wake this loop immediately — you do not wait for the ScheduleWakeup deadline. Arm once; on later iterations call TaskList first and skip this step if a monitor is already running.
3. **At the end of this turn, call ScheduleWakeup** with:
   - `delaySeconds`: with a Monitor armed this is the **fallback heartbeat** — how long to wait if no event fires (lean 1200–1800s; idle ticks past the 5-minute cache window are pure overhead). Without a Monitor this is the cadence — pick based on what you observed. Read the tool's own description for cache-aware delay guidance.
   - `reason`: one short sentence on why you picked that delay.
   - `prompt`: the full original /loop input verbatim, prefixed with `/loop ` so the next firing re-enters this skill and continues the loop. For example, if the user typed `/loop check the deploy`, pass `/loop check the deploy` as the prompt.

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | not-started |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | not-started |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume round-trip | not-started |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; weights sum to 1.0 +/- 1e-3; prints chosen
  slice indices.

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 end-to-end + M8)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Reads simpts from T4 output (or env `SLICE_AT=K`); plugin
  `slice=1000,slice_at=K`; vsim resume + drain; QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: T4 (for simpts) OR explicit SLICE_AT env. F11 (plugin
  slice-consume).
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body; QEMU2 exit 0.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root
  $LOG/snap`; assert `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist + validate
  schema; then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with vsim`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; post-replay state.bin differs from
  archive (PC advanced).

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T2: PC symbol-resolve switching mode (M2).
#
# Same round-trip as T1 but the orchestrator resolves
# _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc from the ELF symbol table and
# threads enter_pc=0x... to the QEMU plugin and --hybrid-exit-pc=0x...
# to vsim. The csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} insns in the binary become inert
# (matchers bypassed; CSR 0x7C0 is a benign vendor write on
# andes-ax45mp). Demonstrates that the same binary can be driven
# without any in-source trigger markers.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)

MODE_LABEL="PC symbol resolve (M2)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: rt_c_v_regs (C-level, V regs + V CSRs)"
echo "Trigger: nm-resolved _hybrid_enter_pc / _hybrid_exit_pc"
echo "=========================================================="

set +e
FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs TRIGGER_MODE=pc EXPECT_RC=0 \
  bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/rt_shared_mem.sh"
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -eq 77 ]; then
  echo "[demo M2] SKIP (artifacts missing)"
  exit 77
fi

if [ "$RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M2] FAIL ($MODE_LABEL): underlying harness exit $RC"
  exit "$RC"
fi

echo "[demo M2] PASS ($MODE_LABEL)"

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | in-progress |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | not-started |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume round-trip | not-started |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral

  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

content.text:
# /loop — schedule a recurring or self-paced prompt

Parse the input below into `[interval] <prompt…>` and schedule it.

## Parsing (in priority order)

1. **Leading token**: if the first whitespace-delimited token matches `^\d+[smhd]$` (e.g. `5m`, `2h`), that's the interval; the rest is the prompt.
2. **Trailing "every" clause**: otherwise, if the input ends with `every <N><unit>` or `every <N> <unit-word>` (e.g. `every 20m`, `every 5 minutes`, `every 2 hours`), extract that as the interval and strip it from the prompt. Only match when what follows "every" is a time expression — `check every PR` has no interval.
3. **No interval**: otherwise, the entire input is the prompt and you'll self-pace dynamically (see "Dynamic mode" below).

If the resulting prompt is empty, show usage `/loop [interval] <prompt>` and stop.

Examples:
- `5m /babysit-prs` → interval `5m`, prompt `/babysit-prs` (rule 1)
- `check the deploy every 20m` → interval `20m`, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 2)
- `run tests every 5 minutes` → interval `5m`, prompt `run tests` (rule 2)
- `check the deploy` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check the deploy` (rule 3)
- `check every PR` → no interval → dynamic mode, prompt `check every PR` (rule 3 — "every" not followed by time)
- `5m` → empty prompt → show usage

## Offer cloud first

Before any scheduling step, check whether EITHER is true:
- the parsed interval (rule 1 or 2) is **≥60 minutes**, or
- regardless of which rule matched, the original input uses daily phrasing ("every morning", "daily", "every day", "each night", "every weekday")

If either is true, call AskUserQuestion first:
- `question`: "This loop stops when you close this session. Set it up as a cloud schedule instead so it keeps running?"
- `header`: "Schedule"
- `options`: `[{label: "Cloud schedule (recommended)", description: "Runs in Anthropic's cloud even after you close this session"}, {label: "This session only", description: "Runs in this terminal until you exit"}]`

If they pick **Cloud schedule**: do NOT call CronCreate. Invoke the `schedule` skill directly via the Skill tool with `args` set to their original input verbatim (e.g. `Skill({skill: "schedule", args: "every morning tell me a joke"})`), then follow that skill's instructions to completion. Do NOT tell the user to run /schedule themselves. **Then stop — do not continue to any section below** (no CronCreate, no ScheduleWakeup, no "execute the prompt now").
If they pick **This session only**:
- If the trigger was a parsed ≥60-minute interval (rule 1 or 2): continue below with that interval.
- If the trigger was daily phrasing only (rule 3, no parsed interval): do NOT call CronCreate. Explain that a daily-cadence loop won't fire before this session closes, so there's nothing useful to schedule locally — suggest they either pick Cloud schedule, or re-run `/loop` with an explicit shorter interval (e.g. `/loop 1h <prompt>`) if they want a session loop. Then stop.
If neither trigger condition was met: continue below.

## Fixed-interval mode (rules 1 and 2)

Convert the interval to a cron expression:

| Interval pattern      | Cron expression     | Notes                                    |
|-----------------------|---------------------|------------------------------------------|
| `Nm` where N ≤ 59   | `*/N * * * *`     | every N minutes                          |
| `Nm` where N ≥ 60   | `0 */H * * *`     | round to hours (H = N/60, must divide 24)|
| `Nh` where N ≤ 23   | `0 */N * * *`     | every N hours                            |
| `Nd`                | `0 0 */N * *`     | every N days at midnight local           |
| `Ns`                | treat as `ceil(N/60)m` | cron minimum granularity is 1 minute  |

**If the interval doesn't cleanly divide its unit** (e.g. `7m` → `*/7 * * * *` gives uneven gaps at :56→:00; `90m` → 1.5h which cron can't express), pick the nearest clean interval and tell the user what you rounded to before scheduling.

Then:
1. Call CronCreate with: `cron` (the expression above), `prompt` (the parsed prompt verbatim), `recurring: true`.
2. Briefly confirm: what's scheduled, the cron expression, the human-readable cadence, that recurring tasks auto-expire after 7 days, and that the user can cancel sooner with CronDelete (include the job ID). Only if you did NOT show the cloud-offer AskUserQuestion above (i.e., neither trigger condition applied), end the confirmation with this exact line on its own, italicized: `_Runs until you close this session · For durable cloud-based loops, use /schedule_`. If the user already answered that question, omit this line.
3. **Then immediately execute the parsed prompt now** — don't wait for the first cron fire. If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.

## Dynamic mode (rule 3 — no interval)

The user wants you to self-pace. Decide what makes the next iteration worth running — a passage of time, or an observable event.

1. **Run the parsed prompt now.** If it's a slash command, invoke it via the Skill tool; otherwise act on it directly.
2. **If the next run is gated on an event** (CI finishing, a log line matching, a file changing, a PR comment) and no Monitor is already running for it: arm one now with `persistent: true`. Its events arrive as `<task-notification>` messages and wake this loop immediately — you do not wait for the ScheduleWakeup deadline. Arm once; on later iterations call TaskList first and skip this step if a monitor is already running.
3. **At the end of this turn, call ScheduleWakeup** with:
   - `delaySeconds`: with a Monitor armed this is the **fallback heartbeat** — how long to wait if no event fires (lean 1200–1800s; idle ticks past the 5-minute cache window are pure overhead). Without a Monitor this is the cadence — pick based on what you observed. Read the tool's own description for cache-aware delay guidance.
   - `reason`: one short sentence on why you picked that delay.
   - `prompt`: the full original /loop input verbatim, prefixed with `/loop ` so the next firing re-enters this skill and continues the loop. For example, if the user typed `/loop check the deploy`, pass `/loop check the deploy` as the prompt.
4. **If you were woken by a `<task-notification>`** rather than this prompt: handle the event in the context of the loop task, then call ScheduleWakeup again with the same `prompt` and the same 1200–1800s `delaySeconds` from step 3 — the Monitor remains the wake signal; this only resets the safety net.
5. **To stop the loop**, omit the ScheduleWakeup call and TaskStop any Monitor you armed (use TaskList to find the task ID if it is no longer in context).
6. Briefly confirm: that you're self-pacing, whether a Monitor is the primary wake signal, that you ran the task now, and what fallback delay you picked.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# F7 contract: QEMU plugin BBV-emit mode. With plugin args
3	# bbv_out=PATH,slice=N the plugin writes one SimPoint-format BBV row
4	# per N retired insns into PATH. Combine with icount=M for a clean
5	# exit at M insns; expected row count = M/N.
6	#
7	# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (tight 2-insn loop; predictable BBV
8	# pattern; CSR-less so no other trigger fires). Plugin must:
9	#   1. Accept bbv_out= and slice= args.
10	#   2. Emit M/N rows into the .bb file before drain.
11	#   3. Each row starts with 'T' (SimPoint .bb format prefix).
12	#   4. The shipped SimPoint binary (tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint) parses
13	#      the file without error.
14	#
15	# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
16	#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
17	#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
18	#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
19	#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary (optional;
20	#                 if missing the SimPoint-parse step is skipped)
21	
22	set -euo pipefail
23	
24	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
25	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
26	TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)
27	
28	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
29	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
30	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
31	SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
32	
33	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
34	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
35	    echo "skip: $f not built"
36	    exit 77
37	  fi
38	done
39	
40	SLICE=1000
41	ICOUNT=10000
42	EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))
43	
44	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
45	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
46	BBV_OUT=$(mktemp -t bbv.XXXXXX.bb)
47	STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
48	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
49	SIMPTS=$(mktemp -t simpts.XXXXXX)
50	WEIGHTS=$(mktemp -t weights.XXXXXX)
51	SP_LOG=$(mktemp)
52	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$BBV_OUT" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG" "$SIMPTS" "$WEIGHTS" "$SP_LOG"' EXIT
53	
54	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
55	
56	set +e
57	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
58	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
59	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
60	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
61	  -nographic \
62	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
63	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
64	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
65	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
66	RC=$?
67	set -e
68	
69	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
70	  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
71	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
72	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
73	  exit 1
74	fi
75	
76	if [ ! -s "$BBV_OUT" ]; then
77	  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: BBV file is empty: $BBV_OUT"

88	fi
89

91	NON_T_ROWS=$(grep -cv '^T' "$BBV_OUT" || true)
92	if [ "$NON_T_ROWS" -ne 0 ]; then
93	  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: $NON_T_ROWS rows do not start with 'T'"
94	  exit 1
95	fi
96	
97	# If SimPoint binary is built, verify it parses the .bb file and
98	# produces non-empty simpts/weights output.
99	if [ -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then

101	  DIM=2
102	  MAXK=2
103	  set +e
104	  "$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim "$DIM" \
105	    -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
106	    >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F7 contract: QEMU plugin BBV-emit mode. With plugin args
# bbv_out=PATH,slice=N the plugin writes one SimPoint-format BBV row
# per N retired insns into PATH. Combine with icount=M for a clean
# exit at M insns; expected row count = M/N.
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (tight 2-insn loop; predictable BBV
# pattern; CSR-less so no other trigger fires). Plugin must:
#   1. Accept bbv_out= and slice= args.
#   2. Emit M/N rows into the .bb file before drain.
#   3. Each row starts with 'T' (SimPoint .bb format prefix).
#   4. The shipped SimPoint binary (tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint) parses
#      the file without error.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary (optional;
#                 if missing the SimPoint-parse step is skipped)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

SLICE=1000
ICOUNT=10000
EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
BBV_OUT=$(mktemp -t bbv.XXXXXX.bb)
STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
SIMPTS=$(mktemp -t simpts.XXXXXX)
WEIGHTS=$(mktemp -t weights.XXXXXX)
SP_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$BBV_OUT" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG" "$SIMPTS" "$WEIGHTS" "$SP_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$BBV_OUT" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: BBV file is empty: $BBV_OUT"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV_OUT")
if [ "$ROWS" -ne "$EXPECTED_ROWS" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: expected $EXPECTED_ROWS BBV rows, got $ROWS"
  head -5 "$BBV_OUT"
  exit 1
fi

# Every row must start with 'T' (SimPoint .bb format prefix).
NON_T_ROWS=$(grep -cv '^T' "$BBV_OUT" || true)
if [ "$NON_T_ROWS" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: $NON_T_ROWS rows do not start with 'T'"
  exit 1
fi

# If SimPoint binary is built, verify it parses the .bb file and
# produces non-empty simpts/weights output.
if [ -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  # For tiny BBVs, both dim and maxK must be conservative.
  DIM=2
  MAXK=2
  set +e
  "$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim "$DIM" \
    -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
    >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
  SP_RC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: simpoint failed to parse BBV (rc=$SP_RC)"
    tail -20 "$SP_LOG"
    exit 1
  fi

  if [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ] || [ ! -s "$WEIGHTS" ]; then
    echo "FAIL[plugin_bbv_emit]: simpoint produced empty output"
    exit 1
  fi
  SP_NOTE="simpoint OK"
else
  SP_NOTE="simpoint not built; parse check skipped"
fi

echo "[plugin_bbv_emit] PASS: rows=$ROWS (target $EXPECTED_ROWS), $SP_NOTE"

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | not-started |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume round-trip | not-started |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T4: BBV-emit switching mode (M4) + SimPoint clustering integration.
#
# QEMU + plugin runs icount_spin.elf with bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M.
# Plugin writes one SimPoint .bb row per N retired insns; expected
# rows = M/N. Then tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint clusters the .bb file
# and produces simpts (slice -> cluster) + weights (cluster -> mass).
#
# Outputs are persisted under $WORKDIR (default: mktemp -d under /tmp)
# so T5/T7 can consume them. WORKDIR is printed on PASS.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF      a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  the built simpoint clustering binary
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir for bbv/simpts/weights (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row (default: 1000)
#   ICOUNT        total insns before drain (default: 10000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2; tiny BBVs need conservative K)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-bbv-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
ICOUNT="${ICOUNT:-10000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"

MODE_LABEL="BBV emit + SimPoint cluster (M4)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: icount_spin (tight 2-insn loop, CSR-less)"
echo "Plugin: bbv_out=...,slice=$SLICE,icount=$ICOUNT"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"
  exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV_OUT="$WORKDIR/icount_spin.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
STATE_OUT="$WORKDIR/state_qemu.bin"
QEMU_LOG="$WORKDIR/qemu.log"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-bbv-shared.XXXXXX)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

EXPECTED_ROWS=$((ICOUNT / SLICE))

echo "[demo M4 1/3] QEMU + plugin: emit BBV ($EXPECTED_ROWS rows expected)"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,icount=$ICOUNT,bbv_out=$BBV_OUT,slice=$SLICE" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $RC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV_OUT")
if [ "$ROWS" -ne "$EXPECTED_ROWS" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: expected $EXPECTED_ROWS BBV rows, got $ROWS"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo M4 2/3] SimPoint cluster: maxK=$MAXK"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV_OUT" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint exit $SP_RC"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ] || [ ! -s "$WEIGHTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo M4] FAIL: simpoint output empty (simpts=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS") weights=$(wc -c <"$WEIGHTS"))"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo M4 3/3] Chosen phases:"
echo "  simpts (slice_idx cluster_id):"
awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$SIMPTS"
echo "  weights (mass cluster_id):"
awk '{printf "    %s\n", $0}' "$WEIGHTS"

# Informational: weights should sum to ~1.0 (no hard assert; simpoint
# may emit weights in unnormalised form for some -maxK paths).
SUM=$(awk '{s+=$1} END{printf "%.4f", s}' "$WEIGHTS")
echo "  weight sum: $SUM"

echo "[demo M4] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): rows=$ROWS, clusters=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS"), workdir=$WORKDIR"

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

|---|---|---|

| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | in-progress |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume round-trip | not-started |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

T1 ---+

T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume round-trip | not-started |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; weights sum to 1.0 +/- 1e-3; prints chosen
  slice indices.

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 end-to-end + M8)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Reads simpts from T4 output (or env `SLICE_AT=K`); plugin
  `slice=1000,slice_at=K`; vsim resume + drain; QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: T4 (for simpts) OR explicit SLICE_AT env. F11 (plugin
  slice-consume).
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body; QEMU2 exit 0.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root
  $LOG/snap`; assert `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist + validate
  schema; then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with vsim`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; post-replay state.bin differs from
  archive (PC advanced).

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# F11 contract: QEMU plugin BBV slice-consume mode. With plugin args
3	# slice=N,slice_at=K the plugin counts slice boundaries (one per N
4	# retired insns) and drains at the end of slice index K (0-based). For
5	# K=2, drain fires at insn 3*N -- the boundary that closes the third
6	# slice. Used by the orchestrator (F8) to chain spawn invocations and
7	# produce one snapshot per SimPoint cluster representative slice.
8	#
9	# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop).
10	# Plugin must:
11	#   1. Accept slice= and slice_at= args.
12	#   2. Drain at insn (slice_at+1)*slice (the END of slice index
13	#      slice_at, 0-based).
14	#   3. Exit 200 (drain) before any icount fallback fires.
15	#
16	# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
17	#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
18	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
19	#   VSIM_ELF     CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
24	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
25	
26	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
27	VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
28	VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
29	
30	for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
31	  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
32	    echo "skip: $f not built"
33	    exit 77
34	  fi
35	done
36	
37	SLICE=1000
38	SLICE_AT=2                                # 0-based: third slice
39	ICOUNT_FALLBACK=10000                     # safety upper bound
40	EXPECTED_MIN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE )) # = 3000
41	
42	SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
43	SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-phase-shared.XXXXXX)
44	STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
45	QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
46	trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT
47	
48	truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"
49	
50	set +e
51	timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
52	  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
53	  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
54	  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
55	  -nographic \
56	  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
57	  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
58	  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
59	  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
60	RC=$?
61	set -e
62	
63	if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
64	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
65	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
66	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
67	  exit 1
68	fi
69	
70	if [ ! -s "$STATE_OUT" ]; then
71	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: state file is empty"
72	  exit 1
73	fi
74	
75	# Verify drain timing via the plugin's own slice counter, not minstret
76	# (which is a cycle-counter proxy on andes-ax45mpv). At drain the plugin
77	# logs `idx=N target=K`; for slice_at=K, N must be K+1 (slices fully
78	# retired). slice_at=2 -> idx=3.
79	EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
80	if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
81	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: did not find drain at idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
82	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
83	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
84	  exit 1
85	fi
86	
87	# Sanity: drained PC should be inside icount_spin's tight loop body
88	# (0x100004 addi, 0x100008 j). Anything outside means drain fired in the
89	# wrong place. pc lives at state-file offset 16 (state_abi_check.h:18).
90	PC=$(python3 -c "
91	import struct
92	with open('$STATE_OUT', 'rb') as f:
93	    f.seek(16)
94	    print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
95	")
96	if [ "$PC" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC" != "0x100008" ]; then
97	  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: drained pc=$PC not in icount_spin loop body"
98	  echo "--- qemu log ---"
99	  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
100	  exit 1
101	fi
102	
103	echo "[plugin_phase_consume] PASS: drain idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC"
104

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F11 contract: QEMU plugin BBV slice-consume mode. With plugin args
# slice=N,slice_at=K the plugin counts slice boundaries (one per N
# retired insns) and drains at the end of slice index K (0-based). For
# K=2, drain fires at insn 3*N -- the boundary that closes the third
# slice. Used by the orchestrator (F8) to chain spawn invocations and
# produce one snapshot per SimPoint cluster representative slice.
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop).
# Plugin must:
#   1. Accept slice= and slice_at= args.
#   2. Drain at insn (slice_at+1)*slice (the END of slice index
#      slice_at, 0-based).
#   3. Exit 200 (drain) before any icount fallback fires.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

SLICE=1000
SLICE_AT=2                                # 0-based: third slice
ICOUNT_FALLBACK=10000                     # safety upper bound
EXPECTED_MIN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE )) # = 3000

SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-phase-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_OUT=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_OUT" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_OUT,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: expected QEMU exit=200, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_OUT" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify drain timing via the plugin's own slice counter, not minstret
# (which is a cycle-counter proxy on andes-ax45mpv). At drain the plugin
# logs `idx=N target=K`; for slice_at=K, N must be K+1 (slices fully
# retired). slice_at=2 -> idx=3.
EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: did not find drain at idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# Sanity: drained PC should be inside icount_spin's tight loop body
# (0x100004 addi, 0x100008 j). Anything outside means drain fired in the
# wrong place. pc lives at state-file offset 16 (state_abi_check.h:18).
PC=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_OUT', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16)
    print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_phase_consume]: drained pc=$PC not in icount_spin loop body"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_phase_consume] PASS: drain idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC"

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T5: slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3) on a CSR-less workload.
#
# Demonstrates that the orchestrator can use DIFFERENT trigger modes for
# the entry and exit of the same vsim window:
#
#   QEMU + plugin: slice=N,slice_at=K -> drains at end of slice K (M5).
#   vsim         : --hybrid-icount M  -> drains after M retired insns (M3).
#
# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop), so neither
# side can use the csrwi 0x7C0 marker. There is no natural QEMU2 exit
# because the workload spins forever; this demo asserts the round-trip
# DRAINS correctly at both ends. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SLICE         insns per slice    (default: 1000)
#   SLICE_AT      0-based slice index for drain (default: 1)
#   ICOUNT_FALLBACK QEMU plugin safety upper bound (default: 10000)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   insns vsim runs before exit drain (default: 5000)
#   SIMPTS        optional simpts file from T4; if set, slice_at picked
#                 from its first row (first column) instead of SLICE_AT env.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
SLICE_AT_ENV="${SLICE_AT:-1}"
ICOUNT_FALLBACK="${ICOUNT_FALLBACK:-10000}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
SIMPTS="${SIMPTS:-}"

# If a SIMPTS file from T4 is provided, pick the first listed slice index.
# Otherwise fall back to SLICE_AT env (default 1).
if [ -n "$SIMPTS" ] && [ -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  SLICE_AT=$(awk 'NR==1{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
  SLICE_AT_SRC="simpts(first row of $SIMPTS)"
else
  SLICE_AT="$SLICE_AT_ENV"
  SLICE_AT_SRC="env SLICE_AT"
fi

MODE_LABEL="Slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: icount_spin (CSR-less tight loop)"
echo "Entry: QEMU plugin slice=$SLICE slice_at=$SLICE_AT (from $SLICE_AT_SRC)"
echo "Exit : vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M5] SKIP: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE ))
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-slice-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[demo M5 1/2] QEMU + plugin: drain at end of slice $SLICE_AT (insn $EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN)"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU state file empty"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify drain happened at the slice boundary (plugin logs idx=K+1 target=K).
EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drain not at slice boundary idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# Drained PC must be inside icount_spin loop body (0x100004 / 0x100008).
PC_Q=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_Q', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drained pc=$PC_Q not in icount_spin loop"
  exit 1
fi
echo "  QEMU drain: idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC_Q"

echo "[demo M5 2/2] vsim: resume + drain after $VSIM_ICOUNT retired insns"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim state file empty"
  exit 1
fi

PC_V=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_V', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC_V" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC_V" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V not in icount_spin loop"
  exit 1
fi
echo "  vsim drain: after $VSIM_ICOUNT insns pc=$PC_V"

echo "[demo M5] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T5: slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3) on a CSR-less workload.
#
# Demonstrates that the orchestrator can use DIFFERENT trigger modes for
# the entry and exit of the same vsim window:
#
#   QEMU + plugin: slice=N,slice_at=K -> drains at end of slice K (M5).
#   vsim         : --hybrid-icount M  -> drains after M retired insns (M3).
#
# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less, predictable tight loop), so neither
# side can use the csrwi 0x7C0 marker. There is no natural QEMU2 exit
# because the workload spins forever; this demo asserts the round-trip
# DRAINS correctly at both ends. M8 is covered by T1 / T2 / T6.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   SLICE         insns per slice    (default: 1000)
#   SLICE_AT      0-based slice index for drain (default: 1)
#   ICOUNT_FALLBACK QEMU plugin safety upper bound (default: 10000)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   insns vsim runs before exit drain (default: 5000)
#   SIMPTS        optional simpts file from T4; if set, slice_at picked
#                 from its first row (first column) instead of SLICE_AT env.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-1000}"
SLICE_AT_ENV="${SLICE_AT:-1}"
ICOUNT_FALLBACK="${ICOUNT_FALLBACK:-10000}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"
SIMPTS="${SIMPTS:-}"

# If a SIMPTS file from T4 is provided, pick the first listed slice index.
# Otherwise fall back to SLICE_AT env (default 1).
if [ -n "$SIMPTS" ] && [ -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  SLICE_AT=$(awk 'NR==1{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
  SLICE_AT_SRC="simpts(first row of $SIMPTS)"
else
  SLICE_AT="$SLICE_AT_ENV"
  SLICE_AT_SRC="env SLICE_AT"
fi

MODE_LABEL="Slice-consume entry (M5) + icount exit (M3)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: icount_spin (CSR-less tight loop)"
echo "Entry: QEMU plugin slice=$SLICE slice_at=$SLICE_AT (from $SLICE_AT_SRC)"
echo "Exit : vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "[demo M5] SKIP: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN=$(( (SLICE_AT + 1) * SLICE ))
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-slice-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[demo M5 1/2] QEMU + plugin: drain at end of slice $SLICE_AT (insn $EXPECTED_QEMU_INSN)"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$SLICE_AT,icount=$ICOUNT_FALLBACK" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU state file empty"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify drain happened at the slice boundary (plugin logs idx=K+1 target=K).
EXPECTED_IDX=$((SLICE_AT + 1))
if ! grep -qE "slice-consume drain \(idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT\)" "$QEMU_LOG"; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drain not at slice boundary idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

# Drained PC must be inside icount_spin loop body (0x100004 / 0x100008).
PC_Q=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_Q', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: QEMU drained pc=$PC_Q not in icount_spin loop"
  exit 1
fi
echo "  QEMU drain: idx=$EXPECTED_IDX target=$SLICE_AT pc=$PC_Q"

echo "[demo M5 2/2] vsim: resume + drain after $VSIM_ICOUNT retired insns"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim state file empty"
  exit 1
fi

PC_V=$(python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$STATE_V', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(16); print(hex(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0]))
")
if [ "$PC_V" != "0x100004" ] && [ "$PC_V" != "0x100008" ]; then
  echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V not in icount_spin loop"
  exit 1
fi
echo "  vsim drain: after $VSIM_ICOUNT insns pc=$PC_V"

echo "[demo M5] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

lines: echo "[demo M5] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V not in icount_spin loop"

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | in-progress |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | not-started |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | not-started |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 end-to-end + M8)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Reads simpts from T4 output (or env `SLICE_AT=K`); plugin
  `slice=1000,slice_at=K`; vsim resume + drain; QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: T4 (for simpts) OR explicit SLICE_AT env. F11 (plugin
  slice-consume).
- Pass criterion: drained PC in icount_spin ROI body; QEMU2 exit 0.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: `orchestrator run examples/csr_oneshot.yaml --archive-root
  $LOG/snap`; assert `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` exist + validate
  schema; then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0 --resume-with vsim`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both steps exit 0; post-replay state.bin differs from
  archive (PC advanced).

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

git -C /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim add tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh && git -C /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: T5 demo_slice_consume_roundtrip mixes M5 entry + M3 exit

Labeled demo showing entry/exit triggers do not have to match. QEMU +
plugin enters vsim via slice=N,slice_at=K (M5); vsim drains via
--hybrid-icount (M3). Workload is icount_spin (CSR-less). Both drained
PCs verified inside the 2-instruction RVC loop body (0x100002 /
0x100004). Accepts a $SIMPTS env hook so T7 (full SimPoint loop) can
feed the slice index chosen by tools/simpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
