#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-200}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-8000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-8000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

191	def replay_checkpoint(
192	    snap_dir: Path,
193	    *,
194	    model: str,
195	    project_root: Path,
196	    work_dir: Path,
197	    artifacts=None,
198	    spawn_timeout: float = 90.0,
199	):
200	    """Hydrate a snap_dir/ archive and resume the chosen model.
201	
202	    Validates `meta.json` via `validate_replay_meta`, allocates a fresh
203	    /dev/shm mmap sized to `meta.mem_size`, overwrites it with
204	    `snap_dir/mem`, copies `snap_dir/state.bin` into `work_dir` as the
205	    spawn's `state_in`, then drives a synthetic 1-step `Session` through
206	    `runner.run_session()` with `initial_state_in` pointing at the copy.
207	
208	    `model='vsim'` -> 1-step `(vsim, csr)` plan; vsim resumes from
209	                      state_in, executes the ROI, drains on its CSR-exit
210	                      trigger, exits 200.
211	
212	    `model='qemu'` -> 1-step `(qemu, end_of_program)` plan; QEMU 2 starts
213	                      halted under gdbstub, the orchestrator restores
214	                      state, then continues to workload completion (rc=0).
215	
216	    Returns the `SessionResult` from `run_session()` unchanged. For vsim,
217	    `state_paths` has exactly one entry (the drained state); for qemu,
218	    `state_paths` is empty (`end_of_program` does not produce a state
219	    file).
220	    """
221	    from orchestrator.mmap_owner import MmapOwner
222	    from orchestrator.plan import Session, Step, TriggerSpec
223	    from orchestrator.runner import Artifacts, run_session
224	
225	    meta = json.loads((snap_dir / "meta.json").read_text())
226	    artifacts = artifacts or Artifacts.from_env(project_root)
227	    live_cpu_variant = _cpu_variant_from_vsim_path(artifacts.vsim)
228	    validate_replay_meta(meta, live_cpu_variant=live_cpu_variant)
229	
230	    mem_size = int(meta["mem_size"])
231	    elf_path = str(meta["elf_path"])
232	
233	    work_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
234	    state_in = work_dir / "replay-state-in.bin"
235	    shutil.copy(snap_dir / "state.bin", state_in)
236	
237	    trigger_kind = "csr" if model == "vsim" else "end_of_program"
238	    if model not in {"vsim", "qemu"}:
239	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported replay model: {model!r}")
240	
241	    with MmapOwner.allocate(mem_size) as shm:
242	        shutil.copy(snap_dir / "mem", shm.path)
243	        synth = Session(
244	            elf=elf_path,
245	            mem_size=mem_size,
246	            mmap_path=str(shm.path),
247	            steps=(Step(

file.content:
def replay_checkpoint(
    snap_dir: Path,
    *,
    model: str,
    project_root: Path,
    work_dir: Path,
    artifacts=None,
    spawn_timeout: float = 90.0,
):
    """Hydrate a snap_dir/ archive and resume the chosen model.

    Validates `meta.json` via `validate_replay_meta`, allocates a fresh
    /dev/shm mmap sized to `meta.mem_size`, overwrites it with
    `snap_dir/mem`, copies `snap_dir/state.bin` into `work_dir` as the
    spawn's `state_in`, then drives a synthetic 1-step `Session` through
    `runner.run_session()` with `initial_state_in` pointing at the copy.

    `model='vsim'` -> 1-step `(vsim, csr)` plan; vsim resumes from
                      state_in, executes the ROI, drains on its CSR-exit
                      trigger, exits 200.

    `model='qemu'` -> 1-step `(qemu, end_of_program)` plan; QEMU 2 starts
                      halted under gdbstub, the orchestrator restores
                      state, then continues to workload completion (rc=0).

    Returns the `SessionResult` from `run_session()` unchanged. For vsim,
    `state_paths` has exactly one entry (the drained state); for qemu,
    `state_paths` is empty (`end_of_program` does not produce a state
    file).
    """
    from orchestrator.mmap_owner import MmapOwner
    from orchestrator.plan import Session, Step, TriggerSpec
    from orchestrator.runner import Artifacts, run_session

    meta = json.loads((snap_dir / "meta.json").read_text())
    artifacts = artifacts or Artifacts.from_env(project_root)
    live_cpu_variant = _cpu_variant_from_vsim_path(artifacts.vsim)
    validate_replay_meta(meta, live_cpu_variant=live_cpu_variant)

    mem_size = int(meta["mem_size"])
    elf_path = str(meta["elf_path"])

    work_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    state_in = work_dir / "replay-state-in.bin"
    shutil.copy(snap_dir / "state.bin", state_in)

    trigger_kind = "csr" if model == "vsim" else "end_of_program"
    if model not in {"vsim", "qemu"}:
        raise ValueError(f"unsupported replay model: {model!r}")

    with MmapOwner.allocate(mem_size) as shm:
        shutil.copy(snap_dir / "mem", shm.path)
        synth = Session(
            elf=elf_path,
            mem_size=mem_size,
            mmap_path=str(shm.path),
            steps=(Step(
                model=model,
                trigger=TriggerSpec(kind=trigger_kind, params={}),
            ),),
        )
        return run_session(
            synth, project_root,
            artifacts=artifacts, spawn_timeout=spawn_timeout,
            work_dir=work_dir,
            initial_state_in=state_in,
        )


def _cpu_variant_from_vsim_path(vsim_bin: Path) -> str | None:
    name = vsim_bin.name
    if name.startswith("sim_"):
        return name[len("sim_"):]
    return None

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T7: full SimPoint pipeline (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7).
#
# Centerpiece demo showing every switching mode in the production
# SimPoint flow:
#
#   M4 - profile pass: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=N -> .bb file
#   ---  cluster      : tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint -loadFVFile ...
#   M5 - per-phase    : QEMU+plugin slice=N,slice_at=K,outfile= -> drain
#   M6 - archive      : write snap/K/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}
#   M7 - replay       : orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim
#
# Workload: rt_c_v_regs (C-level Tier-2 fixture with natural csrwi
# 0x7C0,1 exit marker, so replay's hardcoded vsim+csr trigger drains
# naturally without --hybrid-icount tuning).
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   SIMPOINT_BIN  tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint
#   WORKDIR       persistent dir (default: mktemp -d)
#   SLICE         insns per BBV row / per slice (default: 200)
#   PROFILE_ICOUNT plugin icount fallback for profile pass (default: 8000)
#   PHASE_ICOUNT  plugin icount fallback per phase run (default: 8000)
#   MAXK          simpoint -maxK (default: 2)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$REPO_ROOT/.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
SIMPOINT_BIN="${SIMPOINT_BIN:-$TOP_ROOT/tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint}"
WORKDIR="${WORKDIR:-$(mktemp -d -t demo-simpoint-XXXXXX)}"
SLICE="${SLICE:-200}"
PROFILE_ICOUNT="${PROFILE_ICOUNT:-8000}"
PHASE_ICOUNT="${PHASE_ICOUNT:-8000}"
MAXK="${MAXK:-2}"

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Workload: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "Slice: $SLICE insns; profile icount: $PROFILE_ICOUNT; maxK: $MAXK"
echo "Workdir: $WORKDIR"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done
if [ ! -x "$SIMPOINT_BIN" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: $SIMPOINT_BIN not built"; exit 77
fi
if ! command -v python3 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "[demo T7] SKIP: python3 not found"; exit 77
fi

mkdir -p "$WORKDIR"
BBV="$WORKDIR/profile.bb"
SIMPTS="$WORKDIR/simpts"
WEIGHTS="$WORKDIR/weights"
SP_LOG="$WORKDIR/simpoint.log"
SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))

# ---- M4: profile pass ----------------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 1/4] M4 profile: bbv_out=,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT"
SHM_PROFILE=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-t7-profile.XXXXXX)
PROFILE_STATE="$WORKDIR/profile_state.bin"
PROFILE_LOG="$WORKDIR/profile_qemu.log"
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM_PROFILE"
trap 'rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"' EXIT

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM_PROFILE,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$PROFILE_STATE,bbv_out=$BBV,slice=$SLICE,icount=$PROFILE_ICOUNT" \
  >"$PROFILE_LOG" 2>&1
PRC=$?
set -e
rm -f "$SHM_PROFILE"

if [ "$PRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: profile QEMU expected exit 200, got $PRC"
  tail -20 "$PROFILE_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$BBV" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: BBV file empty"; exit 1
fi
BBV_ROWS=$(wc -l < "$BBV")
echo "  $BBV_ROWS BBV rows -> $BBV"

# ---- M4-cluster: simpoint -----------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 2/4] cluster: simpoint -maxK $MAXK -dim 2"
set +e
"$SIMPOINT_BIN" -loadFVFile "$BBV" -maxK "$MAXK" -dim 2 \
  -saveSimpoints "$SIMPTS" -saveSimpointWeights "$WEIGHTS" \
  >"$SP_LOG" 2>&1
SP_RC=$?
set -e
if [ "$SP_RC" -ne 0 ] || [ ! -s "$SIMPTS" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: simpoint rc=$SP_RC; simpts size=$(wc -c <"$SIMPTS" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
  tail -20 "$SP_LOG"; exit 1
fi
NUM_PHASES=$(wc -l <"$SIMPTS")
echo "  $NUM_PHASES cluster representatives:"
awk '{printf "    slice=%s cluster=%s\n", $1, $2}' "$SIMPTS"

# ---- M5+M6: per-phase drain + archive -----------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 3/4] for each phase: drain at slice_at=K + archive"
PHASE_INDICES=$(awk '{print $1}' "$SIMPTS")
declare -a ARCHIVES=()
for K in $PHASE_INDICES; do
  SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm "vsim-t7-phase${K}.XXXXXX")
  truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"
  STATE="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_state.bin"
  Q_LOG="$WORKDIR/phase_${K}_qemu.log"

  set +e
  timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
    -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
    -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
    -nographic \
    -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
    -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
    -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE,slice=$SLICE,slice_at=$K,icount=$PHASE_ICOUNT" \
    >"$Q_LOG" 2>&1
  QRC=$?
  set -e

  if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
    rm -f "$SHM"
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: phase $K QEMU exit $QRC"; tail -20 "$Q_LOG"; exit 1
  fi

  # M6: write snap/<K>/{state.bin, mem, meta.json} via the orchestrator's
  # write_archive helper (the canonical M6 entry point used by F9).
  SNAP="$WORKDIR/snap/$K"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" python3 - <<PY
from pathlib import Path
from orchestrator.checkpoint import write_archive
write_archive(
    Path("$SNAP"),
    state_path=Path("$STATE"),
    mmap_path=Path("$SHM"),
    mmap_size=$SHM_SIZE,
    elf_path="hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/$(basename "$VSIM_ELF")",
    source_step_index=0,
    cpu_variant="ax45mpv_premium",
    region_id=f"snap/$K",
    slice_index=$K,
)
PY
  AR_RC=$?
  set -e
  rm -f "$SHM"
  if [ "$AR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
    echo "[demo T7] FAIL: write_archive for phase $K (rc=$AR_RC)"; exit 1
  fi
  for f in state.bin mem meta.json; do
    if [ ! -s "$SNAP/$f" ]; then
      echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $SNAP/$f missing/empty"; exit 1
    fi
  done
  ARCHIVES+=("$SNAP")
  echo "  phase $K archived -> $SNAP"
done

# ---- M7: replay each archive --------------------------------------------
echo "[demo T7 4/4] for each archive: replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim"
REPLAY_OK=0
for SNAP in "${ARCHIVES[@]}"; do
  K=$(basename "$SNAP")
  REPLAY_WD="$WORKDIR/replay_$K"
  R_LOG="$WORKDIR/replay_${K}.log"
  mkdir -p "$REPLAY_WD"
  set +e
  PYTHONPATH="$TOP_ROOT/tools" VSIM_QEMU="$VSIM_QEMU" \
    VSIM_PLUGIN="$VSIM_PLUGIN" VSIM_BIN="$VSIM_BIN" \
    timeout 120 python3 -m orchestrator.main replay-checkpoint "$SNAP" \
      --resume-with vsim --project-root "$TOP_ROOT" --workdir "$REPLAY_WD" \
      >"$R_LOG" 2>&1
  R_RC=$?
  set -e
  if [ "$R_RC" -eq 0 ] || [ "$R_RC" -eq 200 ]; then
    echo "  phase $K: replay OK (rc=$R_RC)"
    REPLAY_OK=$((REPLAY_OK + 1))
  else
    echo "  phase $K: replay FAILED (rc=$R_RC)"
    tail -15 "$R_LOG"
  fi
done

if [ "$REPLAY_OK" -ne "$NUM_PHASES" ]; then
  echo "[demo T7] FAIL: $REPLAY_OK/$NUM_PHASES replays succeeded"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[demo T7] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): bbv_rows=$BBV_ROWS phases=$NUM_PHASES replays_ok=$REPLAY_OK workdir=$WORKDIR"

MODE_LABEL="Full SimPoint loop (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | in-progress |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline. Profile pass -> cluster
  -> for each chosen slice: archive that phase -> for each archive: replay
  under vsim.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh` (create)
  - `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/yaml_templates/phase_step.yaml.in` (create)
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created; K replays exit 0; informational
  speedup line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

archive writes call orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive directly;
replays use the orchestrator CLI as F10 ships it.

EOF
)"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# F5 contract: QEMU plugin icount-trigger mode. With plugin arg `icount=N`
# the plugin drains and exits 200 after N retired insns - even on a binary
# that never executes the magic CSR. Foundation for SimPoint slice-consume
# (F11) and the orchestrator's icount-based switching (F8).
#
# Test binary: icount_spin.elf (no csrwi 0x7C0; linked at 0x80000000 for
# andes_ae350). Today's plugin would run forever; the new icount path
# must fire the drain.
#
# Required environment (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU    qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF     a CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built"
    exit 77
  fi
done

ICOUNT_TARGET=100000
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$QEMU_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU,icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: expected QEMU exit=200 on drain, got $RC"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: state file is empty"
  exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at offset 632 (state_abi_check.h:35), little-endian uint64.
MINSTRET=$(python3 -c "
import struct, sys
with open('$STATE_QEMU', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek(632)
    print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
")

if [ "$MINSTRET" -lt "$ICOUNT_TARGET" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[plugin_icount_trigger]: minstret=$MINSTRET < icount=$ICOUNT_TARGET"
  echo "--- qemu log ---"
  cat "$QEMU_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[plugin_icount_trigger] PASS: QEMU exit=$RC, minstret=$MINSTRET (target=$ICOUNT_TARGET)"

| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | not-started |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
  may need different env overrides.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

lines: | T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |

lastPrompt: run /clear then continue with T7

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end + M8 (end-of-program).
#
# Demonstrates that both QEMU1 and vsim can switch via retired-instruction
# count alone, with no CSR markers needed in the workload. QEMU2 still
# resumes via gdbstub and runs to the workload's natural semihosting exit.
#
#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1      -> drain at insn N1 (M3 entry).
#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2       -> drain at insn N2 (M3 exit).
#   QEMU2 via --qemu-binary       -> gdbstub restore, run to exit (M8).
#
# Workload is rt_c_v_regs.elf because it has a natural semihosting exit.
# vsim's --hybrid-icount mode REPLACES the CSR matcher (mutually
# exclusive per main.cpp:482), so the csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} in the binary
# become inert; vsim drains purely on the icount threshold.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 300)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 1000)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-300}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-1000}"

MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3) + end-of-program (M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
echo "QEMU2 trigger: gdbstub restore + run to semihosting exit"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"

echo "[demo M3 1/3] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at state offset 632 (state_abi_check.h).
read_u64() { python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
"; }
MINSTRET_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 632)
PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
if [ "$MINSTRET_Q" -lt "$QEMU_ICOUNT" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 minstret=$MINSTRET_Q < icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"; exit 1
fi
printf "  QEMU1 drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_Q" "$PC_Q"

echo "[demo M3 2/3] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain + spawn QEMU2"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHM_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x80000000 \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

# vsim exits 200 when it drains. If --qemu-binary is set and vsim drains,
# vsim's exec path replaces the process with QEMU2; the final exit code
# we observe is QEMU2's. For rt_c_v_regs that's the semihosting exit
# code (0 on the happy path).
if [ "$VRC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim + QEMU2 chain expected final exit 0, got $VRC"
  tail -40 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  MINSTRET_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 632)
  PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
  printf "  vsim drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_V" "$PC_V"
fi

echo "[demo M3 3/3] QEMU2: semihosting exit reached"
echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 chain via icount triggers"

-plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,$PLUGIN_ARGS" >/dev/null 2>&1
QEMU1_RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QEMU1_RC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 expected exit 200 on drain, got $QEMU1_RC"
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout "$TIMEOUT" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  "${VSIM_TRIG_ARGS[@]}" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

75	VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=()
76	if [ "$TRIGGER_MODE" = "pc" ]; then
77	  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"
78	  VSIM_TRIG_ARGS=(--hybrid-exit-pc "0x$EXIT_PC")
79	fi
80	
81	echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3 mode=$TRIGGER_MODE] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
82	# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
83	# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
84	# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
85	# under either VLEN.
86	# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
87	# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
88	# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
89	# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.

  PLUGIN_ARGS="$PLUGIN_ARGS,enter_pc=0x$ENTER_PC"

fi

# -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512: gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl,
# mmisc_ctl, ...) and pins VLEN to 512 (vlenb=64) so the V state in the
# drain file matches HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX. Non-V fixtures run identically
# under either VLEN.
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native: lets fixtures print from
# QEMU1 (phase 1) via newlib stdio (linked against libgloss_vh), matching
# the QEMU2 launch in qemu_handback.hpp. Phase 2 (vsim) is forbidden from
# doing any syscall -- vsim does not implement semihosting.
# QEMU1 plugin drains state then exits 200 (== kExitHandoffToQemu) to
# distinguish drain from workload completion (0). See F1 in ROADMAP.md.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end + M8 (end-of-program).
#
# Demonstrates that both QEMU1 and vsim can switch via retired-instruction
# count alone, with no CSR markers needed in the workload. QEMU2 still
# resumes via gdbstub and runs to the workload's natural semihosting exit.
#
#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1      -> drain at insn N1 (M3 entry).
#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2       -> drain at insn N2 (M3 exit).
#   QEMU2 via --qemu-binary       -> gdbstub restore, run to exit (M8).
#
# Workload is rt_c_v_regs.elf because it has a natural semihosting exit.
# vsim's --hybrid-icount mode REPLACES the CSR matcher (mutually
# exclusive per main.cpp:482), so the csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} in the binary
# become inert; vsim drains purely on the icount threshold.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 300)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 1000)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-300}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-1000}"

MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3) + end-of-program (M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
echo "QEMU2 trigger: gdbstub restore + run to semihosting exit"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"

echo "[demo M3 1/3] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at state offset 632 (state_abi_check.h).
read_u64() { python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
"; }
MINSTRET_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 632)
PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
if [ "$MINSTRET_Q" -lt "$QEMU_ICOUNT" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 minstret=$MINSTRET_Q < icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"; exit 1
fi
printf "  QEMU1 drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_Q" "$PC_Q"

echo "[demo M3 2/3] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain + spawn QEMU2"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHM_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x80000000 \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

# vsim exits 200 when it drains. If --qemu-binary is set and vsim drains,
# vsim's exec path replaces the process with QEMU2; the final exit code
# we observe is QEMU2's. For rt_c_v_regs that's the semihosting exit
# code (0 on the happy path).
if [ "$VRC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim + QEMU2 chain expected final exit 0, got $VRC"
  tail -40 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  MINSTRET_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 632)
  PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
  printf "  vsim drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_V" "$PC_V"
fi

echo "[demo M3 3/3] QEMU2: semihosting exit reached"
echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 chain via icount triggers"

lastPrompt: run /clear then continue with T7

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end.
#
# Both QEMU1 and vsim switch via retired-instruction count alone, with
# no CSR markers in the workload:
#
#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1    -> drain at insn N1 (M3).
#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2     -> drain at insn N2 (M3).
#
# Workload is icount_spin.elf (CSR-less tight 2-insn RVC loop at 0x100002
# and 0x100004). Neither side could use csrwi 0x7C0 -- icount IS the
# only available drain trigger. There is no natural semihosting exit
# (icount_spin spins forever), so this demo asserts the round-trip
# DRAINS correctly at both ends; M8 is exercised by T1 / T2 / T6.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      CSR-less fixture (default: icount_spin.elf)
#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 10000)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 5000)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/icount_spin.elf}"
QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-10000}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-5000}"

MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF") (CSR-less tight loop)"
echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"

read_u64() { python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
"; }

echo "[demo M3 1/2] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
fi

PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
PC_Q_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_Q")
if [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_Q_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"

echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
fi

PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"

echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 drained pc=$PC_Q_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  QEMU1 drain: pc=$PC_Q_HEX"

echo "[demo M3 2/2] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$VRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim expected exit 200, got $VRC"
  tail -20 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim state empty"; exit 1
fi

PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
PC_V_HEX=$(printf "0x%x" "$PC_V")
if [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100002" ] && [ "$PC_V_HEX" != "0x100004" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim drained pc=$PC_V_HEX not in icount_spin loop"; exit 1
fi
echo "  vsim drain: pc=$PC_V_HEX"

echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): both drains inside icount_spin ROI"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Demo T3: icount switching mode (M3) end-to-end + M8 (end-of-program).
#
# Demonstrates that both QEMU1 and vsim can switch via retired-instruction
# count alone, with no CSR markers needed in the workload. QEMU2 still
# resumes via gdbstub and runs to the workload's natural semihosting exit.
#
#   QEMU1 + plugin icount=N1      -> drain at insn N1 (M3 entry).
#   vsim --hybrid-icount N2       -> drain at insn N2 (M3 exit).
#   QEMU2 via --qemu-binary       -> gdbstub restore, run to exit (M8).
#
# Workload is rt_c_v_regs.elf because it has a natural semihosting exit.
# vsim's --hybrid-icount mode REPLACES the CSR matcher (mutually
# exclusive per main.cpp:482), so the csrwi 0x7C0,{0,1} in the binary
# become inert; vsim drains purely on the icount threshold.
#
# Env (auto-detected with sensible defaults):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   VSIM_ELF      Tier-2 fixture (default: rt_c_v_regs.elf)
#   QEMU_ICOUNT   plugin icount drain target (default: 300)
#   VSIM_ICOUNT   vsim icount drain target  (default: 1000)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/rt_c_v_regs.elf}"
QEMU_ICOUNT="${QEMU_ICOUNT:-300}"
VSIM_ICOUNT="${VSIM_ICOUNT:-1000}"

MODE_LABEL="Icount switching (M3) + end-of-program (M8)"
echo "=========================================================="
echo "Mode: $MODE_LABEL"
echo "Fixture: $(basename "$VSIM_ELF")"
echo "QEMU1 trigger: plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"
echo "vsim  trigger: --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT  (CSR matcher disabled)"
echo "QEMU2 trigger: gdbstub restore + run to semihosting exit"
echo "=========================================================="

for f in "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then echo "[demo M3] SKIP: $f not built"; exit 77; fi
done

SHM_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-icount-rt.XXXXXX)
STATE_Q=$(mktemp)
STATE_V=$(mktemp)
QEMU_LOG=$(mktemp)
VSIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHM" "$STATE_Q" "$STATE_V" "$QEMU_LOG" "$VSIM_LOG"' EXIT
truncate -s "$SHM_SIZE" "$SHM"

echo "[demo M3 1/3] QEMU1 + plugin icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT -> drain"
set +e
timeout 30 "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHM,size=$SHM_SIZE" \
  -machine andes_ae350,memory-backend=mem \
  -cpu andes-ax45mpv,vlen=512 \
  -nographic \
  -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native \
  -bios "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_Q,icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT" \
  >"$QEMU_LOG" 2>&1
QRC=$?
set -e

if [ "$QRC" -ne 200 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 expected exit 200, got $QRC"
  tail -20 "$QEMU_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ ! -s "$STATE_Q" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 state empty"; exit 1
fi

# minstret lives at state offset 632 (state_abi_check.h).
read_u64() { python3 -c "
import struct
with open('$1', 'rb') as f:
    f.seek($2); print(struct.unpack('<Q', f.read(8))[0])
"; }
MINSTRET_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 632)
PC_Q=$(read_u64 "$STATE_Q" 16)
if [ "$MINSTRET_Q" -lt "$QEMU_ICOUNT" ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: QEMU1 minstret=$MINSTRET_Q < icount=$QEMU_ICOUNT"; exit 1
fi
printf "  QEMU1 drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_Q" "$PC_Q"

echo "[demo M3 2/3] vsim --hybrid-icount $VSIM_ICOUNT -> drain + spawn QEMU2"
set +e
timeout 120 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_Q" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_V" \
  --hybrid-icount "$VSIM_ICOUNT" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHM_SIZE" \
  --shared-mem-base 0x00000000 \
  >"$VSIM_LOG" 2>&1
VRC=$?
set -e

# vsim exits 200 when it drains. If --qemu-binary is set and vsim drains,
# vsim's exec path replaces the process with QEMU2; the final exit code
# we observe is QEMU2's. For rt_c_v_regs that's the semihosting exit
# code (0 on the happy path).
if [ "$VRC" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "[demo M3] FAIL: vsim + QEMU2 chain expected final exit 0, got $VRC"
  tail -40 "$VSIM_LOG"; exit 1
fi
if [ -s "$STATE_V" ]; then
  MINSTRET_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 632)
  PC_V=$(read_u64 "$STATE_V" 16)
  printf "  vsim drained: minstret=%s pc=0x%x\n" "$MINSTRET_V" "$PC_V"
fi

echo "[demo M3 3/3] QEMU2: semihosting exit reached"
echo "[demo M3] PASS ($MODE_LABEL): full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 chain via icount triggers"

lastPrompt: run /clear then continue with T7

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | in-progress |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_csr_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none. The underlying e2e already passes in the suite.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: CSR insn match (M1+M8) - PASS".

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Mirror of T1 with `TRIGGER_MODE=pc`. Symbol resolution path
  for `_hybrid_enter_pc` / `_hybrid_exit_pc`.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_pc_oneshot.sh`
- Dependencies: none.
- Pass criterion: exits 0; prints "Mode: PC symbol resolve (M2) - PASS".

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

- Status: not-started
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Drives `icount_spin.elf` through plugin `icount=10000` -> drain
  -> orchestrator -> vsim with `--hybrid-icount 5000` -> drain -> QEMU2 finish.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_icount_roundtrip.sh` (create)
- Dependencies: F5 (plugin icount), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
- Pass criterion: QEMU2 exit 0; drained PC in icount_spin body both times.

### T4 - demo_bbv_profile.sh (M4)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Plugin `bbv_out=PATH,slice=N,icount=M`; runs `tools/simpoint/bin/simpoint`
  on the resulting `.bb` file; parses simpts + weights. Outputs persist
  under `$WORKDIR` so T5/T7 can consume them.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_bbv_profile.sh`
- Dependencies: F7 (BBV emit), F12 (simpoint binary).
- Pass criterion: simpoint exits 0; non-empty simpts + weights; prints chosen
  slice indices and weight sum (informational).

### T5 - demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh (M5 + M3)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Demonstrates trigger-mode mixing on a CSR-less workload.
  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
  may need different env overrides.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.

## Final Integration

After T1-T7 land:
- Add `VSIM_SWITCH_MODE_TESTS` foreach to `hybrid_vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`
  with label `switching-mode`.
- Add `demo` phony target to `hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` invoking
  `demo.sh` which sequentially runs T1..T7.
- Extend `run_e2e.sh` RT_TESTS with the seven demo entries.

| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |

  Entry via plugin `slice=N,slice_at=K` (M5); exit via vsim `--hybrid-icount`
  (M3). Reads simpts from `$SIMPTS` env if set (T4 output); else uses
  `SLICE_AT` env. M8 is covered by T1/T2/T6; icount_spin has no natural
  semihosting exit.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh`
- Dependencies: F11 (plugin slice-consume), F6 (vsim icount trigger).
  T4 optional for SIMPTS env.
- Pass criterion: QEMU drains at slice boundary (idx=K+1 target=K, pc in
  loop); vsim drains after VSIM_ICOUNT insns (pc in loop); both exit 200.

### T6 - demo_archive_replay.sh (M6 + M7)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Inlines a one-step plan YAML (`qemu / kind: csr / on_exit:
  archive_as: snap/0`), runs `orchestrator run --archive-root $WORKDIR`,
  validates `snap/0/{state.bin, mem, meta.json}` (schema=1, abi_magic,
  mem_size=128MB), then `orchestrator replay-checkpoint snap/0
  --resume-with vsim`. Verifies replay PC advanced past archive PC.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_archive_replay.sh`
- Dependencies: F9 (archive emit), F10 (replay).
- Pass criterion: both orchestrator invocations exit cleanly; meta.json
  validates; replay PC differs from archive PC.

### T7 - demo_simpoint_loop.sh (M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7, centerpiece)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Full production SimPoint pipeline on rt_c_v_regs.
  (1) Profile: QEMU+plugin bbv_out=,slice=20,icount=2000 -> 15 BBV rows.
  (2) Cluster: simpoint -maxK 2 -dim 2 -> 2 cluster representatives.
  (3) Per phase: QEMU+plugin slice=20,slice_at=K -> drain; archive via
      `orchestrator.checkpoint.write_archive` Python helper into
      $WORKDIR/snap/K/.
  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`
      hydrates the mmap, vsim drains naturally on csrwi 0x7C0,1.
  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures
  may need different env overrides.
- Key files:
  - `hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_simpoint_loop.sh`
- Dependencies: T4, T5, T6 (this exercises everything they exercise).
  Implementation note: skipped a per-phase YAML template; the
  orchestrator's drain argv builder does not yet thread slice/slice_at,
  so per-phase QEMU drains are direct invocations and archive writes
  use the Python helper directly. Orchestrator IS used for replay.
- Pass criterion: K phase archives created with valid state.bin + mem +
  meta.json; K replays exit 0; final tally line printed.

# TEST ROADMAP: Switching-Mode Demo Suite

Tracks 7 independent demo e2e tests that demonstrate every switching mechanism
(M1-M8) end-to-end. Plan source: `/home/nick/.claude/plans/spicy-juggling-puppy.md`.

## Progress

| Demo | Mode(s) covered | Status |
|---|---|---|
| T1 | M1 CSR insn match + M8 end-of-program | done |
| T2 | M2 PC symbol resolve | done |
| T3 | M3 icount round-trip | done |
| T4 | M4 BBV profile + SimPoint cluster | done |
| T5 | M5 slice-consume + M3 icount exit | done |
| T6 | M6 archive emit + M7 checkpoint replay | done |
| T7 | M4 -> M5 -> M6 -> M7 full SimPoint loop | done |

One session = one demo. Update status when starting (in-progress) and on
completion (done).

## Status Legend

- not-started: demo has not begun
- in-progress: currently being implemented in a session
- done: script exists, passes locally, and CTest entry registered

## Dependency DAG

```
T1 ---+
T2 ---+
T3 ---+
T4 ----> T5 (T5 reuses simpts output)
T4 ----> T7 (T7 invokes the full M4-M5-M6-M7 chain)
T6 ----> T7 (T7 needs archive+replay primitives)
```

T1-T6 parallelise across sessions. T7 is the centerpiece and must land last.

## Demos

### T1 - demo_csr_oneshot.sh (M1 + M8)

- Status: done
- Type: behavioral
- Description: Thin labeled wrapper over `rt_shared_mem.sh FIXTURE=rt_c_v_regs
  TRIGGER_MODE=csr`. Prints a banner naming the mode, invokes the harness,
  reports PASS/FAIL with mode label.

### T2 - demo_pc_oneshot.sh (M2)

### T3 - demo_icount_roundtrip.sh (M3 end-to-end)

  (4) Per archive: `orchestrator replay-checkpoint --resume-with vsim`

  Defaults baked for rt_c_v_regs (SLICE=20, ICOUNT=2000); other fixtures

- Key files:

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# C-level fixtures: standard newlib stdio (printf, fputs, ...) linked
# against the Andes virtual-hosting libgloss (libgloss_vh.a), which
# implements _write / _exit / _sbrk / _read / ... via the RISC-V
# semihosting magic triple. QEMU1 and QEMU2 are launched with
# -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native and intercept the triple
# before it reaches mtvec. vsim does not implement semihosting -- phase
# 2 (the span between enter_vsim and exit_vsim)
# is therefore syscall-free by rule (see runtime/rt_c_helpers.h).
RT_C_FIXTURES    := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_matmul rt_c_static_buf
RT_C_V_FIXTURES  := rt_c_v_regs rt_c_v_csrs rt_c_v_matmul
# Probe fixtures: QEMU1-only, no vsim/QEMU2 phase. Used by shell tests
# that inspect the raw state file to verify plugin drain behavior.
PROBE_V_FIXTURES := probe_v_csr

RUNTIME_DIR     := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS    := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                   $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
# V fixtures additionally need the V regfile boundary helpers
# (v_regfile_reload_all / v_regfile_dump_all). Bodies live in a
# separate TU so the compiler treats each call as opaque and will not
# reorder vector compute across the handoff boundary.
RT_C_V_RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/v_regfile_xfer.c
# -nodefaultlibs suppresses the toolchain's default -lc/-lgloss/-lgcc;
# we re-add them inside --start-group so -lgloss_vh's semihosting stubs
# resolve newlib's _write / _exit / _sbrk / ... before -lgloss (the
# proxy-kernel ecall variant) is even searched. Order matters:
# -lgloss_vh must precede the fallback -lgloss path.
RT_C_LIBS       := -Wl,--start-group -lc -lgloss_vh -lgcc -Wl,--end-group
RT_C_CFLAGS     := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
# RVV-using fixtures need -march=rv64gcv. The crt0 already sets
# mstatus.VS to Initial, so V instructions don't trap.
RT_C_V_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gcv -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                   -nodefaultlibs -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf icount_spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(RT_C_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
     $(PROBE_V_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Switching-mode demo runner: sequentially exercise every M1-M8 mode
# end-to-end via the labeled demo scripts under
# hybrid_vsim/tests/hybrid/demo_*.sh.
#
# Each demo prints its own banner + per-mode PASS/FAIL line. This
# wrapper adds a final tally and propagates a non-zero exit if any
# demo fails. SKIPs (artifacts missing) do not fail the overall run.
#
# Env (auto-detected from repo layout if unset):
#   VSIM_QEMU     qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN   libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_BIN      sim_<cpu>
#   FILTER        regex; only run demos whose name matches
#
# Usage:
#   bash demo.sh                       # all demos
#   FILTER=simpoint bash demo.sh       # just T4 + T7
#   FILTER='csr|pc' bash demo.sh       # T1 + T2

set -uo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
VSIM_DIR=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
TOP_ROOT=$(cd -- "$VSIM_DIR/.." && pwd)
DEMO_DIR="$VSIM_DIR/tests/hybrid"

export VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-$TOP_ROOT/build/qemu/qemu-system-riscv64}"
export VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$TOP_ROOT/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
export VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$VSIM_DIR/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
FILTER="${FILTER:-}"

# Ordered demo list. Each entry: <id>|<demo-script>
DEMOS=(
  "T1|demo_csr_oneshot.sh"
  "T2|demo_pc_oneshot.sh"
  "T3|demo_icount_roundtrip.sh"
  "T4|demo_bbv_profile.sh"
  "T5|demo_slice_consume_roundtrip.sh"
  "T6|demo_archive_replay.sh"
  "T7|demo_simpoint_loop.sh"
)

cat <<EOF
============================================================
Hybrid Sim Switching-Mode Demo Suite
============================================================
Repo root : $TOP_ROOT
QEMU      : $VSIM_QEMU
Plugin    : $VSIM_PLUGIN
vsim      : $VSIM_BIN
Filter    : ${FILTER:-<none>}
============================================================
EOF

PASS=0; FAIL=0; SKIP=0
declare -a RESULTS=()
START_TS=$(date +%s)

for entry in "${DEMOS[@]}"; do
  IFS="|" read -r ID SCRIPT <<<"$entry"
  if [ -n "$FILTER" ] && ! echo "$SCRIPT" | grep -qE "$FILTER"; then
    continue
  fi
  printf "\n--- %s : %s ---\n" "$ID" "$SCRIPT"
  if [ ! -x "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT" ]; then
    printf "  MISSING: %s\n" "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT"
    RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  MISSING")
    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
    continue
  fi
  bash "$DEMO_DIR/$SCRIPT"
  RC=$?
  case "$RC" in
    0)  PASS=$((PASS + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  PASS") ;;
    77) SKIP=$((SKIP + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  SKIP") ;;
    *)  FAIL=$((FAIL + 1)); RESULTS+=("$ID  $SCRIPT  FAIL(rc=$RC)") ;;
  esac
done

END_TS=$(date +%s)
ELAPSED=$((END_TS - START_TS))

printf "\n============================================================\n"
printf "Demo Suite Summary  (elapsed %ds)\n" "$ELAPSED"
printf "============================================================\n"
for line in "${RESULTS[@]}"; do
  printf "  %s\n" "$line"
done
printf "  ----\n"
printf "  PASS=%d  FAIL=%d  SKIP=%d\n" "$PASS" "$FAIL" "$SKIP"

if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
  exit 1
fi
exit 0

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/nick/work/hybrid_sim/hybrid_vsim/hybrid/test/demo.sh
